payload.description:
Goal: Extract OpenClaw context management behind a pluggable core abstraction while preserving current behavior.

Scope:
- Define a neutral, OpenClaw-native context execution contract (no LCM-specific APIs in core).
- Route runtime context execution through that contract:
  - ingest
  - assemble
  - compact
- Re-implement current behavior via a legacy/default engine implementation using the new abstraction.
- Keep existing compaction UX/event contracts intact (/compact behavior, auto-compaction signaling, compaction counters).

Non-goals:
- No mandatory retrieval/lineage semantics in core interface.
- No behavior changes for users unless explicitly behind config/feature selection.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Existing context behavior is preserved when default engine is selected.
2) Existing tests remain in place and passing.
3) New tests are added for abstraction boundaries and engine selection behavior.
4) A plugin-capable selection path exists for alternate context engines.

payload.description:
## Bug

`LcmContextEngine.ingest()` in `src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts:106-113` calls `JSON.stringify(message.content)` when content is an array of content blocks (e.g. `[{type:"text", text:"HEARTBEAT_OK"}]`). This stores the raw JSON string in the DB.

When the assembler later reconstructs messages from the DB, the model sees literal JSON like `[{"type":"text","text":"HEARTBEAT_OK"}]` as its own prior output and starts pattern-matching on it — producing corrupted responses that echo raw JSON content block syntax.

## Root Cause

The content extraction in `ingest()` has a branch for non-string content that blindly JSON.stringify's it:

```typescript
const content =
  "content" in message
    ? typeof message.content === "string"
      ? message.content
      : JSON.stringify(message.content)  // ← this line
    : ...
```

AgentMessage `.content` is frequently an array of content blocks like `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, {type: "thinking", ...}]`. The text should be extracted, not serialized.

## Fix

Replace the `JSON.stringify` branch with content block extraction:

```typescript
: Array.isArray(message.content)
  ? (message.content as { type?: string; text?: string }[])
      .filter((b) => b.type === "text" && typeof b.text === "string")
      .map((b) => b.text)
      .join("\n")
  : JSON.stringify(message.content)
```

Keep `JSON.stringify` as final fallback for unexpected shapes.

## Files

- `src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts` — fix content extraction in `ingest()` method

## Tests Required

Add tests in the LCM test suite that verify:
1. String content is stored as-is
2. Content block arrays `[{type:"text", text:"hello"}]` are flattened to `"hello"`
3. Mixed content blocks (text + thinking + tool_use) extract only text blocks
4. Empty arrays produce empty string
5. Non-array non-string content falls back to JSON.stringify
6. Roundtrip: ingest a message with content blocks, then verify the stored DB content is plain text (not JSON)
payload.title: LCM ingest corrupts content block arrays via JSON.stringify
LCM assemble() replaces live session messages with incomplete DB, causing context amnesia

payload.description:
Make LCM the sole context manager when contextEngine=lcm. Currently LCM is a passive observer — it ingests post-prompt messages but never replaces live session context. This epic covers the full path to active context management:

1. Bootstrap existing session history into LCM DB on first activation
2. Capture all messages (system prompts, user messages) during ingest
3. Wire real LLM-powered summarization for compaction
4. Add threshold-based proactive compaction triggers
5. Disable pi-coding-agent's internal auto-compaction when LCM is active
6. Re-enable assemble() to build context from the canonical LCM store

Spec document: /tmp/lcm-active-context-manager-spec.md

Parent epic: openclaw-79e (LCM plugin)
Depends on: openclaw-7c4 (ingest content fix) and openclaw-4d9 (assemble pass-through fix)
payload.title: LCM Active Context Manager — replace pi-coding-agent context lifecycle

payload.description:
When LCM is enabled on an existing session, the DB is empty — no history from before activation. On the first ingest/assemble call for a session with no conversation in the DB, read the session JSONL file and bulk-ingest its messages.

## How session files work

pi-coding-agent's SessionManager.open(sessionFile) parses JSONL where each line is a JSON object with id, parentId, timestamp, type fields. Entry types: message, compaction, thinking_level_change, model_change, etc. Message entries have role + content in AgentMessage shape. Compaction entries contain summary text that replaced earlier messages.

## Implementation

- Add a bootstrap(params: { sessionId, sessionFile }) method to LcmContextEngine
- Call from attempt.ts before the first assemble() call, passing the session file path
- Parse JSONL, filter to message-type entries, map to AgentMessage format
- Bulk-ingest into conversation store
- Track bootstrap state (e.g. bootstrapped flag in conversations table) so it runs once per session
- Handle compaction entries: ingest as summary records, not raw messages
- Add bootstrap to ContextEngine interface as optional method

## Files

- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts — add bootstrap() method
- src/plugins/lcm/store/conversation-store.ts — bulk insert support
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts — call bootstrap before assemble
- src/context-engine/types.ts — add optional bootstrap to interface

## Caveats

- JSONL format is internal to pi-coding-agent — keep parser isolated
- Session file path already available in attempt pipeline (sessionFile param)
- Large sessions may take time to bootstrap — consider async/progress
payload.title: Bootstrap existing session JSONL into LCM DB on first activation

payload.description:
Current ingest only captures messagesSnapshot.slice(prePromptMessageCount) — post-prompt new messages. It misses system prompts (set via agent.setSystemPrompt(), not part of message array) and potentially user messages.

## How system prompts work

AgentSession.prompt() calls agent.setSystemPrompt(baseOrModifiedPrompt) before agent.prompt(messages). The system prompt is NOT part of the messages array. Extensions can modify it via before_agent_start hook.

## How user messages work

The user message is built inside AgentSession.prompt() and passed as part of the messages array to agent.prompt(messages). Need to verify whether pi-coding-agent appends these to the session's message list (making them visible in activeSession.messages post-prompt) — if so, the current ingest slice captures them. If not, we have a gap.

## Implementation

- System prompts: ingest as role="system" message at conversation start, track hash to detect changes and re-ingest only when modified
- User messages: verify current path captures them. If not, explicitly ingest before prompt call
- DB schema already supports MessageRole = "user" | "assistant" | "system" | "tool"
- Consider dedup logic for system prompts (same content shouldn't create duplicate entries)

## Files

- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts — add system prompt ingestion
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts — wire system prompt capture
- src/plugins/lcm/store/conversation-store.ts — dedup logic for system prompts
payload.title: Full ingest coverage — capture system prompts and user messages

payload.description:
LcmContextEngine.compact() uses a placeholder summarizer (createDefaultSummarize in engine.ts) that just truncates text to ~3600 chars. Real compaction requires LLM-powered summarization.

## Current state

- compact() accepts a summarize callback via legacyParams.summarize
- If not provided, falls back to createDefaultSummarize() which truncates
- The /compact command path passes legacyParams with provider/model info but no summarize callback
- Summarize signature: (text: string, aggressive?: boolean) => Promise<string>

## Implementation

- Create summarization prompt templates (normal: preserve key details; aggressive: distill to essentials)
- Wire the configured LLM provider into the summarize callback using provider/model from legacyParams
- Ensure provider info flows through from both /compact and overflow-retry compact paths
- Consider token budget for the summarization call itself

## Files

- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts — wire real summarizer using provider from legacyParams
- src/plugins/lcm/summarize.ts — new file for prompt templates and provider call
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts — ensure provider info flows through

payload.description:
pi-coding-agent fires compaction proactively when context exceeds a threshold (checked pre-prompt and post-turn). LCM compact only runs on overflow retry and /compact. If we disable internal compaction, we lose proactive compaction.

## How pi-coding-agent threshold works

_checkCompaction() checks after each turn and before each prompt. Reads last assistant message usage.totalTokens, compares against context window. Two triggers: overflow (error-driven, retries) and threshold (proactive, no retry).

## Implementation

- After each ingest() call, evaluate conversation total tokens against threshold
- If over LcmConfig.contextThreshold (default 0.75) of token budget, trigger compact()
- Token budget comes from model context window (available via legacyParams or session config)
- Should be post-ingest, non-blocking to the prompt response path
- Consider: should this run after every ingest, or only after the full post-prompt ingest batch?

## Files

- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts — add threshold evaluation after ingest
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts — pass token budget, add post-ingest compaction check

## Implementation

- Use existing override point in src/agents/pi-settings.ts
- LcmConfig.autocompactDisabled field already exists but isn't wired — use it
- Guard: only disable when all prerequisites are met (bootstrap done, real summarizer wired, threshold trigger active)

- Raw JSONL parsing is insufficient: `parseSessionEntries()` only parses/skips malformed lines and does not perform migration/index semantics (`.../session-manager.js:78`, `.../session-manager.js:93`).
- Runtime context is tree/leaf-path based (`buildSessionContext()` in `.../session-manager.js:107`, `.../session-manager.js:579`), so bootstrap must not ingest abandoned branches.
- Current schema has no bootstrapped marker (`src/plugins/lcm/db/migration.ts:5`).
- Store currently lacks bulk insert API (`src/plugins/lcm/store/conversation-store.ts:175`).
- `prepareSessionManagerForRun()` may truncate files with no assistant entries (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/session-manager-init.ts:44`), so bootstrap ordering must avoid data loss.

Scope
1. Add `bootstrap(params: { sessionId, sessionFile })` to `LcmContextEngine` (`src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts`) and optional `bootstrap` to `ContextEngine` interface (`src/context-engine/types.ts`) if needed.

4. Add bulk insert API in `ConversationStore` for efficient initial import.
5. Wire bootstrap call in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` before LCM assemble activation and before any truncation-prone init path.
6. Include idempotency and duplicate protection so bootstrap is one-time per session.

Acceptance criteria
- First LCM activation on an existing session imports only the active leaf-path conversation, not abandoned branches.
- Repeat activation does not duplicate imported messages.
- Bootstrap state is persisted in DB and visible in code-level checks.
- Bulk import path is used (not per-message loop only).

payload.description:
## Goal
Harden current LCM expansion behavior to make it bounded, auditable, and authorization-ready for delegated traversal.

## Why this exists
Current implementation has three concrete gaps:
1. `lcm_expand` can run effectively unbounded when `tokenCap` is omitted in the agent tool path.
2. Compaction rewrites context but does not emit durable compaction event parts in `message_parts`.
3. `ExpansionAuthManager` exists but is not wired into runtime usage.

These are prerequisites for safe, automatic multi-hop subagent expansion.

## Scope
This epic delivers only runtime hardening and guardrails. It does **not** implement full automatic route-and-spawn orchestration policy.

## Deliverables
- Enforced default + max cap behavior for `lcm_expand`
- Durable compaction event part writes with token/accounting metadata
- Runtime grant issuance/validation plumbing for delegated expansion

## Non-goals
- No product-level auto-orchestration policy changes (handled in separate epic)
- No DB backend migration (SQLite remains the intended backend)
payload.title: LCM: expansion hardening (caps, compaction audit events, auth grants)

payload.description:
## Problem
`lcm_expand` defaults are inconsistent by entrypoint:
- Plugin helper path (`buildExpansionToolDefinition`) defaults to `config.maxExpandTokens`.
- Agent tool path (`createLcmExpandTool`) leaves `tokenCap` undefined, which propagates to orchestrator as effectively unbounded (`Infinity`).

This can produce oversized expansions and context blowups, especially in subagent deep traversal.

## Current code references
- `src/agents/tools/lcm-expand-tool.ts`
- `src/plugins/lcm/expansion.ts`
- `src/plugins/lcm/db/config.ts` (`maxExpandTokens`)

## Required behavior
1. If caller omits `tokenCap`, apply default from LCM config (`maxExpandTokens`).
2. If caller supplies `tokenCap`, clamp to an explicit max (same config value unless a separate hard cap is introduced).
3. Keep existing behavior for query-based and direct-summary expansion, except now always bounded.
4. Preserve backward-compatible response shape (`content`, `details`).

## Implementation notes
- Ensure the cap logic is applied in **all** runtime entrypoints for expansion (not only helper utilities).
- Keep failure behavior and error messages stable unless clearly improving correctness.
- Do not silently widen limits beyond configured bounds.

## Acceptance criteria
- Omitting `tokenCap` in `lcm_expand` produces bounded expansion using configured default.
- Oversized requested `tokenCap` is clamped (or rejected with clear error, if that design is chosen and documented).
- Existing lcm expansion tests pass; add tests proving bounded defaults/clamping in agent tool path.

## Test plan
- Unit tests for cap resolution logic in expansion tool path.
- Regression tests for both query and summary-id expansion modes.
- Run relevant LCM + tool tests via Vitest.

## Out of scope
- Automatic subagent spawn policy.
payload.title: LCM: enforce bounded default/clamped token caps for lcm_expand

payload.description:
## Problem
Compaction mutates context graph state (summaries/lineage/context_items) but does not emit durable compaction event parts into `message_parts`.

The spec intent is to retain an auditable operation trail for each compaction pass.

## Current code references
- `src/plugins/lcm/compaction.ts`
- `src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts` (compact call flow)
- `src/plugins/lcm/store/conversation-store.ts` (message/message_parts APIs)
- `src/plugins/lcm/db/migration.ts` (`part_type=compaction` exists in schema)

## Required behavior
1. On each successful compaction action (leaf and/or condensed pass), write a compact event record into canonical history.
2. Persist event as `message_parts.part_type=compaction` with metadata sufficient for auditability.
3. Event metadata should include at minimum:
   - `conversationId`
   - compaction level (`normal|aggressive|fallback`)
   - `tokensBefore`, `tokensAfter`
   - created summary id(s)
   - whether condensed pass occurred
4. Ensure event writes are resilient and do not corrupt compaction flow on partial failures.

## Design constraints
- Canonical data remains append-first and non-destructive.
- Do not re-architect compaction algorithm in this task.
- Keep event write semantics deterministic and testable.

## Acceptance criteria
- Compaction run produces at least one `message_parts` row with `part_type=compaction`.
- Stored metadata includes token deltas + summary references.
- Existing compaction behavior remains functionally unchanged except event emission.

## Test plan
- Add tests asserting compaction event part creation and metadata fields.
- Cover leaf-only and leaf+condensed scenarios.
- Run LCM engine/integration tests.

## Out of scope
- Adding external metrics pipeline wiring.
payload.title: LCM: emit durable compaction event parts in canonical history

payload.description:
## Problem
`ExpansionAuthManager` and `wrapWithAuth` exist but are not wired into runtime flows. Delegated/subagent expansion therefore has no enforced grant boundaries in production code paths.

## Current code references
- `src/plugins/lcm/expansion-auth.ts`
- `src/plugins/lcm/expansion.ts`
- `src/agents/tools/lcm-expand-tool.ts`
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts`

## Required behavior
1. Introduce runtime grant lifecycle for delegated expansion:
   - grant creation by parent/orchestrator context
   - grant propagation to delegated run
   - grant validation in expansion execution path
2. Enforce at least these grant constraints:
   - allowed conversation scope
   - optional allowed summary ID scope
   - max depth
   - max token cap
   - expiry + revocation handling
3. Ensure non-delegated/local expansion still works without unnecessary friction.

## Implementation guidance
- Prefer a small integration surface first; avoid over-design.
- Use existing `ExpansionAuthManager` semantics unless a concrete runtime mismatch requires adaptation.
- Keep error messages actionable (authorization failure should explain which constraint failed).
- If a transport mechanism is needed (session metadata, tool params, or both), document and test it.

## Acceptance criteria
- Delegated expansion attempts without valid grant are rejected.
- Delegated expansion with valid grant succeeds within grant limits.
- Expired/revoked grants fail with clear errors.
- Existing direct local use-cases remain functional.

## Test plan
- Integration tests for grant create/use/fail paths.
- Include depth/token cap and conversation scope violations.
- Run LCM + sessions_spawn related tests.

## Out of scope
- Full automatic orchestration policy (separate epic).

payload.description:
## Goal
Implement full automatic spec-style LCM expansion orchestration:
- main agent routes via describe/grep
- system auto-delegates deep traversal to subagents when appropriate
- subagents return distilled findings with cited IDs

## Why this exists
Today, subagent-based expansion is possible manually (using `sessions_spawn` + LCM tools), but not automatically orchestrated as a first-class runtime behavior.

## Scope
Deliver policy + runtime orchestration + validation for autonomous route-and-delegate expansion loops.

## Prerequisites
This epic assumes hardening work is complete:
- bounded expansion caps
- compaction audit events
- grant-based authorization for delegated traversal

## Success criteria
- Main agent automatically chooses direct vs delegated expansion based on objective risk/complexity signals.
- Delegated expansion remains bounded and auditable.
- User-facing responses are concise; raw expansion payloads do not flood top-level context.
payload.title: LCM: full automatic spec-style subagent expansion orchestration

payload.description:
## Objective
Define and implement deterministic orchestration policy for when the main agent should:
1. answer directly,
2. do shallow/direct `lcm_expand`, or
3. spawn delegated subagent traversal.

## Required decision model
Implement explicit trigger heuristics (documented + tested), including at least:
- traversal depth requested/estimated
- number of candidate summary IDs
- estimated token risk vs configured caps
- broad time-range / multi-hop retrieval indicators

## Required output contract
- Parent run should receive concise synthesis + cited IDs.
- Avoid dumping full raw expansion payload into top-level context.

## Files likely touched
- `src/agents/...` orchestration or tool-invocation layer
- `src/plugins/lcm/expansion.ts` (if helper contracts need extension)
- system prompt/tool guidance files if needed

## Acceptance criteria
- Policy logic is explicit (not purely prompt magic) and unit-tested.
- Decision outcomes are explainable in logs/debug metadata.
- No regression for existing manual `sessions_spawn` workflows.

## Test plan
- Unit tests for policy decisions across representative scenarios.
- Include boundary cases near token/depth thresholds.
payload.title: LCM Orchestration: implement deterministic route-vs-delegate policy

payload.description:
## Objective
Implement end-to-end automatic orchestration runtime so deep LCM expansion can happen without manual `sessions_spawn` choreography.

## Required behavior
1. Main agent identifies candidate IDs via describe/grep.
2. If policy selects delegation, system spawns subagent with narrow task and bounds.
3. Subagent performs bounded expansion (under grants + caps).
4. Subagent returns distilled findings + cited IDs.
5. Main agent optionally performs additional focused passes or final response synthesis.

## Required safeguards
- Enforce grant scope for delegated expansions.
- Enforce token/depth bounds throughout all passes.
- Ensure cleanup/archive behavior for spawned subagent sessions remains correct.

## Integration points
- `sessions_spawn` path and subagent lifecycle
- LCM expansion tool execution
- parent/child messaging and announce flow

## Acceptance criteria
- Automatic delegated expansion works end-to-end with no manual subagent invocation.
- Final top-level response stays concise and user-oriented.
- Failure modes (timeout, auth denial, no matches) degrade gracefully.

## Test plan
- E2E tests covering route-only, single delegated pass, and multi-pass follow-up.
- Timeout/error-path tests for child runs.
- Regression tests for existing session tool behavior.
payload.title: LCM Orchestration: wire automatic delegated expansion runtime loop

payload.description:

payload.description:
## Bug
Some sessions contain assistant messages where `content` is a string (legacy or reconstructed history).
`@mariozechner/pi-ai` assumes assistant content is always array-shaped and calls `assistantMsg.content.flatMap(...)`, which throws:

`assistantMsg.content.flatMap is not a function`

## Repro
- Send a follow-up prompt in a session where assistant history contains string `content`
- Observe error surfaced back to chat

## Scope
- Seen in runtime path during history sanitize / context assemble before next model call

## Proposed fix
Normalize assistant message content to array text blocks before model handoff and after context assembly output.

## Notes
Reported via screenshot from Telegram chat; message text was repeated as runtime error output.
payload.title: Assistant content shape crash in pi-ai transformMessages

payload.description:
The LCM context engine proactive compaction fails with 'Error: database is not open'. The SQLite connection is getting closed/disposed prematurely between agent turns, so when compact() is called the DB handle is stale.

Logs show repeated failures:
[agent/embedded] context engine proactive compact failed: Error: database is not open

The system falls back to legacy Pi safeguard compaction, so not catastrophic, but LCM summaries are never created.

Root cause likely in LcmContextEngine.dispose() calling closeLcmConnection() too early — possibly at end of each agent turn instead of at gateway shutdown. Or the connection singleton is shared and closed by one consumer while another still needs it.

Key files:
- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts (dispose method)
- src/plugins/lcm/db/connection.ts (connection singleton)
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts (where dispose is called)
payload.title: LCM proactive compact fails: database is not open

payload.description:
## Bug
When LCM is enabled, each run resolves a new `LcmContextEngine` instance but `db/connection.ts` used a single process-global sqlite handle.
`run.ts` always calls `contextEngine.dispose()` at end of run. In concurrent runs, one run finishing can close the shared DB while another run is still ingesting/compacting, causing:

- `context engine ingest failed: Error: database is not open`
- `context engine proactive compact failed: Error: database is not open`

## Fix
Use ref-counted connections keyed by db path in `src/plugins/lcm/db/connection.ts`, and release by path in `LcmContextEngine.dispose()`.

## Validation
Add regression test in `src/plugins/lcm/engine.test.ts` to verify one engine disposal does not break another active engine using the same DB path.
payload.title: LCM shared sqlite handle can close during concurrent runs

payload.description:
Improve lcm_grep, lcm_expand, and lcm_describe for practical agent use:

1. **lcm_grep: time filters + recency sort**
   - Add since/before parameters (ISO timestamps)
   - Return timestamps with each result
   - Default sort by recency (most recent first)

2. **lcm_expand: recency bias in query mode**
   - When using query-based expansion, bias toward more recent summaries
   - Drop the hard requirement for conversationId when query is provided — infer from session

3. **Auto-scope to current session's conversation**
   - All three tools should default to the current agent's conversation, not search across all agents
   - The agent runtime should pass conversationId implicitly so the tools don't require it as a parameter
   - Cross-conversation search should still be possible with an explicit flag

Context: Currently lcm_grep returns 20 results with no timestamps or ordering, making it hard to find recent context. lcm_expand query mode errors without conversationId. Agents should be looking at their own history by default.
payload.title: LCM retrieval: add time filters, recency sorting, and auto-scoping

payload.description: After /compact, the reported token count (e.g. '48k → 1.7k') only reflects LCM conversation data (summaries + messages). It excludes system prompts, workspace files, and skills — making 'Context 1.7k/200k (1%)' misleadingly low. Fix: estimate system prompt overhead and add to tokensAfter in commands-compact.ts.

LCM ingests all heartbeat tool call/result pairs into canonical context where legacy engine did not (legacy ingest was a no-op). Each heartbeat poll adds ~16k tokens of worker snapshot data. Fix: detect heartbeat turns in LCM ingestBatch/ingestSingle and either skip them entirely or mark them as ephemeral/low-priority so they don't accumulate in assembled context.

Key files:

- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts (ingestBatch at :998, isHeartbeat flag available from :995)

TDD approach: Write tests first that verify heartbeat messages are NOT present in assembled context after ingest. Verify tests fail on current code, then implement the fix, verify tests pass.
payload.title: Filter heartbeat turns from LCM ingest

payload.description:
LCM compaction threshold decision undercounts real assembled payload. extractMessageContent misses large non-text/raw metadata blocks during tokenization, but the assembler rehydrates those full blocks. Observed: context_items token sum was 136k but actual reconstructed payload was ~357k tokens. Compaction threshold (75% of 204k = 153k) never triggers despite context being way over.

Key files:
- src/plugins/lcm/compaction.ts (:82, :89 - threshold decision using getContextTokenCount)
- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts (:108 - extractMessageContent for tokenization)  
- src/plugins/lcm/assembler.ts (:99, :151 - rehydrates raw blocks from message_parts.metadata.raw)
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts (:189 - estimateSessionTokenCount)

TDD approach: Write tests that create messages with large raw/metadata blocks, verify getContextTokenCount returns accurate counts matching what the assembler would produce. Verify tests fail on current code, then fix extractMessageContent/tokenization to account for raw metadata, verify tests pass.
payload.title: Fix LCM compaction token accounting mismatch

payload.description:
Per the LCM paper (Ehrlich & Blackman, 2026), lcm_expand should be restricted to sub-agents only. The main agent should not be able to expand summaries inline in its context window.

**Rationale:** Expanding compacted summaries in the main interaction loop risks context flooding — you compact to save space, then immediately re-expand and blow the context back up. The paper restricts expand to sub-agents so that expansion happens in an isolated context window, and only the relevant findings are returned to the parent.

**Current behavior:** The main agent can call lcm_expand on any summary with no restriction. The expansion-policy routing may delegate heavy traversals to sub-agents, but this is an optimization, not a hard restriction. The only protection is a token cap (default 4000 tokens).

**Desired behavior:**
- Main agent cannot call lcm_expand directly
- Main agent can only observe summaries in its context (via lcm_describe and lcm_grep)
- When the main agent needs to drill into a summary, it delegates to a sub-agent via the existing expansion grant system
- Sub-agents can call lcm_expand with proper grants (already implemented)

**Implementation notes:**
- The grant system in expansion-auth.ts already exists for sub-agent scoping
- The delegation loop in lcm-expand-tool.delegation.ts already handles sub-agent expansion
- Main change: add a guard in lcm-expand-tool.ts that rejects calls from non-subagent sessions
- Consider providing a helpful error message pointing the agent to use lcm_describe instead, or to delegate via a sub-agent task

This becomes more important with incremental compaction (many small summaries), where expanding several small summaries could snowball context usage.
payload.title: Restrict lcm_expand to sub-agents only

payload.description:
The LCM soft leaf trigger (evaluateLeafTrigger + compactLeafAsync) is currently wired in src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts alongside the hard trigger. This is a pi-embedded-runner file that can conflict when upgrading pi.

Ideally all LCM compaction logic lives inside the LCM plugin. Options explored:
1. Pass a summarizer factory or configureSoftCompaction(params) at engine init — still requires a one-liner in attempt.ts but moves all logic into the engine
2. Use an after_ingest hook from the pi-embedded-runner
3. Have the engine own the summarizer construction (needs provider/model/apiKey plumbing at construction time)

The core challenge: the summarizer needs provider/model/apiKey which are only known at runtime in attempt.ts, not at engine construction time. Any solution still touches attempt.ts minimally.

Defer to a cleanup pass once incremental compaction is working satisfactorily.
payload.title: Refactor: move LCM soft trigger out of pi-embedded-runner attempt.ts

payload.description:
Implement large file handling in the LCM plugin. When a file block (>25k tokens) enters via ingestSingle(), intercept it before persistence: store the raw content to disk, generate an exploration summary, insert into the existing large_files table, and rewrite the message content with a compact ~200 token reference (file ID + exploration summary). This prevents large files from blowing up active context.

Spec: specs/lcm-large-file-handling.md

Key implementation points:
- Interception in engine.ts ingestSingle(), after toStoredMessage() but before createMessage()
- Parse <file> blocks via regex, check token count against threshold (25k default)
- Store raw content at ~/.openclaw/lcm-files/<conv_id>/<file_id>.<ext>
- Exploration summary: deterministic for code/structured data, LLM for unstructured text
- File IDs propagate through compaction via existing file_ids column on summaries
- New file: src/plugins/lcm/large-files.ts (parsing, formatting, exploration)
- Config: largeFileTokenThreshold added to LcmConfig
- lcm_describe already handles file_ IDs — no changes needed there

All existing storage infra (large_files table, insertLargeFile, getLargeFile, lcm_describe rendering) is already built. This wires up the missing ingestion path.
payload.title: LCM: Large file interception at ingest

payload.description:
When the soft trigger fires compactLeafAsync from attempt.ts, it never passes previousSummaryContent. The plumbing exists all the way down (compactLeafAsync -> compactLeaf -> leafPass -> summarizeWithEscalation), and the full sweep path (compactFullSweep) correctly chains previousSummaryContent between successive leaf passes.

The fix: in compactLeaf (compaction.ts), before running the leaf pass, fetch the 1-2 most recent summary context items that precede the chunk being compacted and pass their content as previousSummaryContent. This way each soft leaf pass knows what conversation preceded the messages it's summarizing, producing better continuity in the summary DAG.

The full sweep path already handles this correctly by chaining leafResult.content between passes. The gap is only in the single-pass soft trigger path where there's no prior pass to chain from.

Note: freshTailCount was just bumped from 16 to 32 in a separate commit.
payload.title: LCM: soft leaf compaction should include prior summaries as continuity context

payload.description:
Messages from a complete request/response turn were lost during a gateway restart. The turn completed (delivered to Telegram) but was never persisted to LCM. Seq numbers jump from 591→592 in conversation 553 with no gap — the entire turn (user message, assistant response with tool calls finding/fixing idempotencyKey bug, multiple assistant messages) is absent from the DB.

Root cause investigation needed:
1. Trace when LCM ingest happens relative to turn completion — is it async/deferred?
2. Find the code path: where does the session's in-memory message state get flushed to LCM?
3. Determine if ingest is batched on a timer, triggered by next turn, or something else
4. Propose a fix: either sync persistence at end-of-turn, or flush-on-shutdown hook, or both

Key files to start with:
- src/plugins/lcm/engine.ts (ingestSingle, the main ingest path)
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts (where compaction/LCM calls happen per-turn)
- src/gateway/ (shutdown hooks, session persistence)

Evidence: conversation 553, messages between seq 591 and 592. The full turn was visible in Telegram but never written to lcm.db.
payload.title: LCM: messages lost on gateway restart — ingest not flushed before shutdown

payload.description:
Current Phase 2 condensation mixes leaves and condensed nodes freely, producing degenerate linked-list trees. Oldest content gets re-summarized N times while newest content is summarized once. Expand cost grows linearly with conversation length.

Fix: only condense nodes at the same depth. Leaves merge with leaves → depth-1 nodes. Depth-1 nodes merge together → depth-2 nodes. Produces balanced trees with uniform information loss and predictable O(log N) expand cost.

Changes:
- Add depth column to summaries table (0 for leaves, max(child)+1 for condensed)
- Replace selectOldestCondensedChunk with selectOldestChunkAtDepth
- Phase 2 loop: shallowest eligible depth first, break on depth mismatch
- New config: condensedMinFanout (default 4), condensedMinFanoutHard (default 2)
- Migration: backfill depth from DAG for existing summaries

Spec: specs/lcm-depth-aware-condensation.md
payload.title: LCM: depth-aware condensation to produce balanced summary trees

payload.description: Add 'depth INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0' to summaries table. Migration: all kind='leaf' get depth=0 (via DEFAULT). All kind='condensed' need DAG walk via summary_parents to compute max(parent.depth)+1. Fallback for parentless condensed: depth=1. Small graph walk, runs inline in migration. Keep kind field for backwards compat.
payload.title: Schema: add depth column to summaries table + migration backfill

payload.description: Replace selectOldestCondensedChunk with selectOldestChunkAtDepth. Key difference: breaks on depth mismatch when chunk already started (don't mix depths). Takes targetDepth param, filters to summaries at that depth only. Also add getDistinctDepthsInContext helper (SELECT DISTINCT s.depth FROM context_items ci JOIN summaries ... ORDER BY depth ASC). Both need to respect freshTailOrdinal.
payload.title: New selection: selectOldestChunkAtDepth + getDistinctDepthsInContext

payload.description: Replace current Phase 2 condensation with depth-aware algorithm. Loop: find shallowest eligible depth (ascending scan), check fanout threshold (leafMinFanout=8 for depth 0, condensedMinFanout=4 for depth>=1), select oldest chunk at that depth, condense to depth+1. Restart from shallowest after each condensation. Terminate when no eligible depth or no token reduction. Hard trigger and /compact use condensedMinFanoutHard=2 for all depths.
payload.title: Depth-aware Phase 2 loop in compactFullSweep

payload.description: Add three new LCM config parameters: leafMinFanout (default 8, min depth-0 nodes to trigger condensation), condensedMinFanout (default 4, min same-depth nodes at depth>=1), condensedMinFanoutHard (default 2, relaxed minimum for hard triggers and /compact). Wire into compaction engine. Update config schema and validation.
payload.title: Config: add leafMinFanout, condensedMinFanout, condensedMinFanoutHard

payload.description: When summarizing leaves into depth-1 nodes, include neighboring leaf content in prompt for context (existing openclaw-098 feature). When summarizing condensed nodes (depth>=1) into higher-depth nodes, do NOT include neighboring node content — each condensed node is already self-sufficient. Check the summarization prompt path and gate continuity context inclusion on depth==0.
payload.title: Skip continuity context for condensed node summarization

payload.description: Unit tests: depth assignment (leaves=0, condensed=max(child)+1), selectOldestChunkAtDepth only returns matching depth, depth break stops chunk on mismatch, shallowest-first processing, fanout threshold enforcement (leaf vs condensed). Integration: multiple compaction rounds produce balanced DAG, migration backfill correctness, interleaved leaf+sweep workload stays balanced, lcm_expand traverses balanced tree correctly. Regression: soft trigger unchanged, full sweep reduces below threshold, fresh tail protection.
payload.title: Tests: depth-aware condensation unit + integration

payload.description:
completeSimple() in the LCM summarizer path returns input text verbatim in ~200ms without making a real API call. This causes buildDeterministicFallbackSummary() to truncate, producing garbage summaries ending with '[LCM fallback summary; truncated for context management]'.

Evidence: conv 553 has 5/71 fallback summaries, including ALL 4 condensed (depth>0) summaries. Leaf summaries mostly work (66/71 good). The condensed path uses reasoning:'high' while leaf does not — likely the distinguishing factor.

Sting's e2e test reproduces it: src/plugins/lcm/engine.e2e-real.test.ts (on sting-bf630a62 worktree branch). Run: RUN_E2E=1 LIVE=1 npx vitest run src/plugins/lcm/engine.e2e-real.test.ts

Root cause is in completeSimple — model resolution and API key lookup succeed but the actual API call never fires. Investigate whether reasoning:'high', the system prompt, or model ID handling causes the short-circuit.
payload.title: completeSimple silently returns input verbatim for condensed summaries

payload.body: Context from Josh: likely introduced with depth-aware condensation (openclaw-b47/d2f). The condensed summarizer path is the new code — it adds reasoning:'high' and uses buildCondensedSummaryPrompt(). Leaf summaries (which don't use reasoning:'high') mostly work fine. Manual /compact also triggers the condensed pass. Start investigation there — the reasoning parameter or the condensed prompt builder are prime suspects.

payload.description:
Goal
Add a dedicated OpenClaw/lossless-claw config option to choose provider/model specifically for delegated subagent runs spawned by lcm_expand_query, independent from the default model chain.

Why
Today lcm_expand_query delegated child sessions do not set model explicitly before gateway.agent, so expansion quality/cost cannot be tuned independently from default session model selection.

Scope
- Config/schema for dedicated lcm_expand_query delegated model selection.
- Deterministic precedence across runtime override, env override, config, and existing fallback chain.
- Tool/runtime wiring to apply resolved model to child session before child run.
- Tests + docs.

Acceptance Criteria
- lcm_expand_query can resolve a dedicated child model using defined precedence.
- When configured, delegated child session runs with configured model/provider.
- When unset, behavior remains backward compatible.
- Invalid model selections fail clearly and do not start child runs.
payload.title: LCM expand_query: configurable delegated child model selection

payload.description:

Scope

- Add `agents.list[].subagents.lcmExpandQuery.model`.

- Add schema/help/labels coverage and tests.

Acceptance Criteria

payload.title: Config/schema: add lcm_expand_query delegated model option

payload.description:
Implement runtime resolution + application of dedicated lcm_expand_query delegated model.

Scope
- Add resolver helper in model-selection module.
- Precedence: runtime param > env override > per-agent config > defaults config > existing fallback chain.
- In lcm_expand_query delegated path, patch child session model before `gateway.agent`.

Acceptance Criteria

payload.description: When a heartbeat handler generates a response with user-relevant content (e.g. worker completion notifications), the message is swallowed and never delivered to Telegram. v2026.2.26-beta.1 introduced directPolicy defaulting to block. Evidence: Worker Adele completed a task, heartbeat generated a completion summary, gateway log shows sendMessage ok but message_id corresponds to a different message (/status response). The worker notification never appeared in Telegram. directPolicy: allow was set but the message was already lost. Investigation needed: trace heartbeat response lifecycle, directPolicy filtering path, message delivery queue ordering.
payload.title: Heartbeat responses with user-relevant content not delivered to Telegram

payload.body:
Investigation findings for heartbeat delivery suppression:

Root cause (high confidence)
- Delivery suppression happens before outbound send, at target-resolution time, not at HEARTBEAT_OK classification time.
- `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` returns `channel: "none", reason: "dm-blocked"` when the resolved destination is direct/DM and `heartbeat.directPolicy === "block"`.
  - Code: `src/infra/outbound/targets.ts:333-339`.
- `runHeartbeatOnce` still executes the model call, but later hard-skips delivery whenever `delivery.channel === "none" || !delivery.to`.
  - Code: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:861-870`.
- Result: content-bearing heartbeat replies are dropped the same as HEARTBEAT_OK acks if target resolution returns `none` (for example `dm-blocked`), so worker-completion summaries never reach Telegram DM.

Where HEARTBEAT_OK vs content is handled
- HEARTBEAT token stripping/suppression is separate and later:
  - `stripHeartbeatToken` (ack classification): `src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts:122-170`
  - `normalizeHeartbeatReply` / `shouldSkipMain`: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:780-795`
- That means the DM block gate can swallow non-ack content before outbound delivery, independent of HEARTBEAT_OK semantics.

Lifecycle trace (exact path)
1) Heartbeat run starts and resolves delivery target (`resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget`).
2) If DM + `directPolicy:block`, target is rewritten to `channel:none` with `reason:dm-blocked`.
3) Model still runs (`getReplyFromConfig`) and payload is normalized.
4) Before send, `runHeartbeatOnce` checks `delivery.channel`; if `none`, emits skipped event and returns.
5) `deliverOutboundPayloads` is never called, so nothing is queued/sent for Telegram.

Queue-order hypothesis check
- Very unlikely this was queued behind `/status` and then dropped. In the `channel:none` branch, outbound delivery is never invoked at all (`deliverOutboundPayloads` is bypassed), so there is no heartbeat message to queue.
- This matches the observed behavior where a nearby `sendMessage ok` can belong to another command while the heartbeat result never appears.

Version note (date-specific)
- In tag `v2026.2.26-beta.1` (2026-02-26), source shows DM block is conditional (`&& heartbeat?.directPolicy === "block"`) and changelog states default is `allow` again.
- So if DM content was suppressed in that build, the most likely trigger is an effective runtime config path with `directPolicy:block` (global/per-agent/overrides), not HEARTBEAT_OK filtering.

Recommended fix approach
1) Clarify policy semantics in code:
- If intended behavior is “suppress only HEARTBEAT_OK in DMs, deliver real content”, move DM policy enforcement after payload classification, not in target resolution.
- Keep DM target resolved, classify reply, then block only ack-like payloads.

2) If current behavior is intentional (block all DM heartbeat sends):
- Keep logic but make it explicit in operator UX/logs by including `reason=dm-blocked` with preview in user-facing diagnostics.
- Add/expand tests proving that content replies are intentionally skipped when `directPolicy:block`.

3) Config UX hardening:
- Add a startup warning when heartbeat target resolves to DM while `directPolicy:block`, since this silently drops all heartbeat content for that route.
Investigation update: identified additional non-policy suppression gates in heartbeat pipeline; details shared in worker report.

payload.description:
Three bugs cause real content in heartbeat replies to be silently discarded:

1. DM policy gate (targets.ts:333-339): resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget resolves to channel:none when directPolicy===block, hard-skipping delivery BEFORE classifying whether reply has real content.

2. Last-payload selection (heartbeat-reply-payload.ts:3-22): Discards all but last non-empty payload. [worker update, HEARTBEAT_OK] -> only HEARTBEAT_OK survives.

3. Ack classifier (auto-reply/heartbeat.ts:110-170): ackMaxChars:300 threshold drops content-bearing messages containing HEARTBEAT_OK.

Fix: Move DM policy enforcement AFTER classification. Only suppress true ack-like payloads. Content-bearing replies must always be delivered.
payload.title: Fix heartbeat delivery suppression of content-bearing replies

Investigation found that commentary-phase assistant text is stored intentionally in transcript history for replay, but two live user-visible surfaces bypass the phase-aware filters.

Evidence/path:
- src/agents/openai-ws-message-conversion.ts stores commentary text blocks with textSignature phase metadata and can combine them with toolCall blocks in one assistant message.
- src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.ts persists that assistant message verbatim and emits SessionTranscriptUpdate with the raw message.
- src/gateway/server-session-events.ts forwards update.message directly as session.message without sanitizeChatHistoryMessages/extractAssistantVisibleText.
- src/gateway/server-chat.ts forwards any assistant agent event with data.text into chat deltas/finals without checking data.phase.
- chat.history and sessions history SSE already sanitize commentary-phase text, so the leak is inconsistent across surfaces.

Likely fix: apply the same phase-aware suppression/sanitization in server-session-events and server-chat, plus regression tests for commentary+toolCall assistant turns.
payload.title: Suppress commentary-phase assistant text in live chat/session.message surfaces

payload.description: Observed on current main after updating from v2026.4.15. The main agent is configured for openai-codex/gpt-5.4 with embeddedPi.executionContract=strict-agentic and full tools. Symptom: model replies that it is about to do or retry work, but no tool call appears in verbose mode and no action occurs. Evidence: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts at HEAD now passes tools: builtInTools from splitSdkTools(), and splitSdkTools() returns builtInTools: []. In @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent 0.68.1, createAgentSession() maps options.tools to both initialActiveToolNames and allowedToolNames. AgentSession then filters the entire tool registry, including customTools, through allowedToolNames. An empty tools list therefore disables all tools, not just built-ins. Commit bee2e0f38f explicitly worked around this by passing a sessionToolAllowlist derived from allowed tool names; commit d7ea136384 reverted to tools: builtInTools. The stricter GPT-5 prompt contract and strict-agentic execution make the regression more visible because the model is encouraged to act and follow through, but the runtime has no active tools. Relevant refs: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts, src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts, src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-split.ts, node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/sdk.js, node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/agent-session.js, commits bee2e0f38f and d7ea136384.
payload.title: Embedded Pi GPT-5 runs can narrate actions without executing tools

payload.description: Intermittent Telegram streamed replies can remain visible with a trailing ellipsis even though the model run and session transcript contain the complete final answer. Evidence from 2026-05-16 Lossless Claw topic turn: session d1f293a4-840d-4283-8f85-3e51aff00d77 has a full 1445-char final assistant message with stopReason=stop, while Telegram displayed the draft ending at "live runtime prompt overhead...". Current likely gap: the transcript-backed recovery in lane-delivery-text-deliverer only works when the full transcript candidate is readable during final delivery; if delivery receives an ellipsis snapshot before that candidate is available, it can update/stop the active preview and mark it finalized. Scope: extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts, extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts, and the embedded subscriber final/block reply handoff. Test approach: add a regression where final text ends with ellipsis, transcript lookup initially misses, and delivery must not commit the truncated stream snapshot when a complete final answer is available from the turn result or a later durable source.

payload.description:

- RFC defines hot transcripts as those referenced by live session entries. Legacy transcript files not referenced by any live entry are archive-tier and should not be parsed into hot SQLite rows.
- Current doctor reports unreferencedJsonlFiles but leaves them in the legacy sessions directory.
- Operationally, leaving archive-tier files at the old active path can mask missed runtime readers/writers. The current branch already archives imported hot transcript files outside sessions/ into session-sqlite-import-archive.

Evidence from current branch:
- src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts imports referenced transcripts into SQLite.
- src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:501 reports unreferenced JSONL files.
- src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:520 archives only imported referenced transcript files plus adjacent trajectory sidecars.
- src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:575 uses a sibling session-sqlite-import-archive directory outside sessions/.

Acceptance:
- Decide whether unreferenced archive-tier JSONL should remain in place, move to an archive directory, or be cataloged differently.
- If moved, preserve support/archive discoverability while ensuring active runtime paths cannot read them accidentally.
- Include backup/reversibility rules for local/operator migrations.
- Add doctor inspect/fix tests for stale archive-tier files and imported hot files.

payload.description:
RFC #7 requires live runtime transcript events to use SQLite rows after the storage flip. JSONL files are allowed only as migration inputs or archive/export artifacts.

Problem:
`src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` still has a live transcript-file path for injected/webchat/source-reply assistant messages:
- `resolveTranscriptPath` at `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1744` resolves a filesystem transcript path using `resolveSessionFilePath`;
- `ensureTranscriptFile` at `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1766` creates a JSONL header/file when missing;
- `findAssistantTranscriptMessageByIdempotencyKey` and `findSourceReplyTranscriptMirrorByMetadata` scan JSONL indexes at `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1800` and `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1865`;
- `appendAssistantTranscriptMessage` appends through `appendInjectedAssistantMessageToTranscript` at `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1936`;
- live call sites remain around `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:3910`, `4556`, `4804`, and `5200`.

Impact:
This preserves active session file behavior after the SQLite flip. A migrated install can continue creating or relying on active `.jsonl` transcript files instead of SQLite transcript rows, which violates the one-way migration contract and risks split-brain transcript state.

Required fix:
Route these gateway chat persistence paths through the SQLite/session-accessor transcript identity APIs. Do not create fallback JSONL files for active sessions. Source-reply mirror lookup/rewrite/idempotency must work against SQLite transcript rows. Keep archive/export file behavior out of the live runtime path.

Validation:
Add focused coverage showing injected assistant/webchat/source-reply persistence writes/reads SQLite transcript rows and does not create active JSONL files. Run the narrow relevant tests plus `node scripts/check-session-transcript-reader-boundary.mjs`.
payload.title: Stop gateway chat live JSONL transcript writes

payload.description:
RFC #7 names compaction as part of the live runtime path that must read/write SQLite rows after the flip. Manual compaction must not discover active transcript files or pass filesystem `sessionFile` as the compaction target.

Problem:
`src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` still preserves the pre-flip file path:
- `sessions.compact` reads/mutates session state with `updateSessionStore` at `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:2418`;
- when `maxLines` is omitted, it calls `resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates(...).find((candidate) => fs.existsSync(candidate))` at `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:2443`;
- it passes `sessionFile: filePath` into `compactEmbeddedAgentSession` at `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:2494`;
- on success it writes `result.result.sessionFile` back into the entry at `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:2552`.

Impact:
Manual compaction can fail or silently skip a valid migrated session because no JSONL file exists, or it can preserve/update file metadata for active runtime state. That is a direct half-measure against the SQLite-only runtime contract.

Required fix:
Use the session accessor/SQLite transcript identity path for manual compaction preflight, compaction execution, checkpoint metadata, and post-compaction session-entry updates. The compaction runtime should receive enough session identity to operate on rows, not a live transcript file path. Do not add fallback file discovery for active sessions.

Validation:
Add/update manual compaction tests for a SQLite-only session with no active JSONL transcript. Verify compaction reads rows, writes rows/checkpoints, updates the session entry, and leaves no active transcript file requirement.
payload.title: Move manual sessions.compact off transcript files

payload.description:
RFC #7 keeps existing file-shaped SDK imports only during the pre-SQLite deprecation window. At the storage flip, active transcript file APIs should disappear from TypeScript exports, generated baselines, docs, and package entrypoints. Archive artifacts can still exist, but not as active transcript runtime APIs.

Problem:
`src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts` still exports explicit file-target helpers:
- `SessionTranscriptFileTargetParams` / `SessionTranscriptFileTarget` at `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts:63`;
- `resolveSessionTranscriptFileTarget` at `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts:139`;
- `publishSessionTranscriptFileUpdate` at `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts:275`;
- `withSessionTranscriptFileWriteLock` at `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts:338`;
- `readSessionTranscriptFileEvents` streams and parses JSONL at `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts:369`.

The generated API baseline confirms these remain public from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime`.

Impact:
External and bundled plugin code can continue building new active behavior around transcript files after the flip. That turns archive/file implementation details into a durable SDK contract and weakens the one-way migration.

Required fix:
Remove the file-target transcript runtime exports from the post-flip SDK surface. Replace internal/bundled callers with identity-based transcript APIs. If any archive/export helper is truly still needed, make it explicitly archive/support-scoped and not part of the active runtime transcript writer/update surface. Refresh generated API baselines and docs.

Validation:
Run the plugin SDK API baseline generation/checks used by this repo, plus focused plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime tests. Confirm no public `SessionTranscriptFileTarget*`, `resolveSessionTranscriptFileTarget`, `publishSessionTranscriptFileUpdate`, or `withSessionTranscriptFileWriteLock` remains in the active SDK baseline.
payload.title: Remove transcript-file targets from post-flip plugin SDK

payload.description:
RFC #7 says runtime session paths, including compaction, should use stable identity rather than transcript file paths after the SQLite flip. The public context-engine delegate still exposes and forwards `sessionFile`.

Problem:
`src/context-engine/delegate.ts` keeps a file-shaped compaction bridge:
- `delegateCompactionToRuntime` is exported at `src/context-engine/delegate.ts:34`;
- it forwards `sessionFile: params.sessionFile` into `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirect` at `src/context-engine/delegate.ts:58`;
- it returns `result.result.sessionFile` at `src/context-engine/delegate.ts:80`;
- the generated SDK baseline exposes this from both `openclaw/plugin-sdk` and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`.

Impact:
Even if gateway compaction is fixed, the public SDK still tells context engines that compaction is file-targeted. That preserves old active session-file semantics as an SDK contract.

Required fix:
After the manual compaction/runtime seam is identity-shaped, update `ContextEngine["compact"]`, `ContextEngineRuntimeContext`, `delegateCompactionToRuntime`, generated baselines, and internal callers so compaction is keyed by session identity/agent/store context rather than active transcript file. Do not leave throwing stubs or compat aliases for the old `sessionFile` parameter unless a tagged shipped SDK contract is explicitly cited and approved.

Validation:
Run focused context-engine/plugin-sdk type/API baseline checks and compaction delegate tests. Confirm generated baselines no longer expose `sessionFile` for the compaction delegate/result active path.
payload.title: Replace context-engine compaction sessionFile contract

payload.description:

payload.description:

Analysis:
This is the likely root surface for many downstream failures. Treat active session metadata and transcript state as SQLite rows only. Do not restore sessions.json fallback behavior. Existing tests that assert file storage should be deleted or rewritten, but tests that assert product-visible metadata/session lifecycle behavior should be made to pass through the SQLite accessor.

Acceptance:
- active session entry write/update/delete operations preserve required metadata, timestamps, usage/cost/model/route fields, terminal lifecycle fields, and ACP metadata behavior in SQLite;

payload.description: Follow-up work from the SQLite marker sweep after fixing overflow tool-result truncation. Active session state is SQLite-only; sessionFile may be a sqlite:* marker and must not be treated as a live JSONL path except for archive/import/export/support artifacts that explicitly operate on files. This epic tracks the remaining product gaps and cleanup surfaces where code still assumes active sessionFile is a filesystem transcript.
payload.title: Path 3 SQLite marker file-path follow-ups

payload.description: Affected area: extensions/active-memory/index.ts and embedded recall runs. The plugin still builds a JSONL sessionFile for the recall subagent and then reads transcript state through streamBoundedTranscriptJsonl(), fs.stat(), and createReadStream(). It uses that state for terminal memory-search detection, timeout partial replies, search debug, usable/unavailable memory-result flags, and persisted plugin status. Under the SQLite flip, active runtime sessionFile values may be sqlite:* markers, and active state should be read via SQLite/accessor APIs rather than direct JSONL streaming. Current behavior is likely degraded rather than crashing because read failures are swallowed, but timeout/debug/fallback decisions can lose evidence. Design direction: prefer a storage-neutral transcript read helper or SDK/runtime projection; do not add active JSONL fallback. Tests should cover SQLite marker/session rows for timeout partial reply, terminal memory-search result detection, and searchDebug extraction.
payload.title: Active Memory: route SQLite recall transcripts through accessors

payload.description: Affected area: src/agents/btw.ts and src/agents/btw-transcript.ts. /btw uses an in-memory active-run snapshot when available, but if that is missing it resolves a transcript with resolveSessionFilePath() and then readBtwTranscriptMessages() parses the path with fs.readFile(). Under SQLite-only active sessions, resolveSessionFilePath() can return a sqlite:* marker or no useful JSONL artifact, so /btw can lose prior context or report 'No active session context.' Product behavior: /btw side questions should inherit the selected visible parent conversation branch, including fork/leaf behavior, whether the active session is backed by SQLite or an archive file. Design direction: resolve a storage-neutral transcript target and read visible/branch-selected events via accessor/projection APIs; keep file reading only for explicit file/archive artifacts. Tests should cover SQLite transcript rows, selected leaf/branch behavior, no active-run snapshot fallback, and exclusion of the current triggering user turn where applicable.
payload.title: /btw: read prior context from SQLite transcripts

payload.description: Affected area: src/commands/export-trajectory.ts, src/trajectory/command-export.ts, and src/trajectory/export.ts. The sessions export-trajectory command resolves sessionFile, checks pathExists(sessionFile), then exportTrajectoryBundle() stat()s and parses that file. Under the SQLite flip, active sessionFile can be a sqlite:* marker and there may be no JSONL transcript path, so trajectory export likely fails with 'Session file not found' or a stat/read failure. Product behavior: /export-trajectory and openclaw sessions export-trajectory should export the active session trajectory from canonical SQLite transcript rows, while still supporting named file artifacts where explicitly provided. Design direction: introduce a storage-neutral export input or materialize an export-local JSONL artifact from SQLite rows inside the export operation; do not restore steady-state JSONL session files. Tests should cover CLI/request path for SQLite session entries, branch-visible transcript export, runtime event inclusion, and file artifact export where still intended.
payload.title: Trajectory export: support SQLite-backed sessions

payload.description: Affected area: src/agents/run-session-target.ts and callers of runEmbeddedAgent() that still pass hand-built sessionFile paths. resolveAgentRunSessionTarget() currently prioritizes an explicit sessionFile over sessionTarget/store identity, which lets active callers force a JSONL-backed target even on the SQLite flip. Some one-off/internal helper runs intentionally create temporary transcript artifacts, but active session/product paths should identify the run through sessionTarget/sessionId/sessionKey and let the accessor resolve SQLite. The main observed risk is Active Memory, which manufactures a recall JSONL session file and then reads it directly. Design direction: audit explicit sessionFile callers, keep truly isolated artifact runs documented, and move active product callers to sessionTarget/accessor identity. This should probably be done before or together with the Active Memory fix so that the plugin does not reintroduce file-backed active runtime state.
payload.title: Embedded run targets: stop active callers forcing file-backed sessionFile

payload.description: Affected area: src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts, src/agents/session-file-repair.ts, src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts, and nearby attempt setup. Several active attempt lifecycle probes still fingerprint, repair, prewarm, or inspect params.sessionFile as a concrete JSONL path. Under SQLite markers most of these paths catch ENOENT and become harmless no-ops, while SessionManager.open() and the write lock are already marker-aware. This is lower severity than the product gaps because it does not appear to reproduce the overflow ENOENT crash, but it preserves stale active file-store behavior and can create misleading fence/repair/bootstrap decisions. Design direction: skip these file-only probes for SQLite markers or replace them with accessor-backed checks where the product behavior is still needed. Do not restore sessions.json/JSONL active fallback. Tests should verify SQLite marker attempts avoid file repair/prewarm/fence file reads while preserving file-artifact behavior where still supported.

payload.title: SQLite sessions/transcripts flip (PR #98236): review findings

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (doctor-migration agent F1 + production agent F1; both independently confirmed). Severity: BLOCKER.

**Defect**: The gateway runs `runDoctorSessionSqlite({mode:"import", allAgents:true})` on EVERY boot (`src/gateway/server-startup-session-migration.ts:65-70`); targets are selected purely by `fs.existsSync(target.storePath)` (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:174-182`). Nothing ever tombstones/renames/archives `sessions.json` after a validated import (doctor only moves `.jsonl` files, `doctor-session-sqlite.ts:811-862`). Post-flip the runtime writes only SQLite, so `sessions.json` is frozen at migration time — and re-imported forever.

The only re-import guard is `markAlreadyMigratedTranscript` (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:423-435, 649-668`), which requires (a) transcript file missing AND (b) exact sessionId equality. `importSqliteSessionRows` is a blind upsert with no freshness/updatedAt guard (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:1222-1269`; `writeSessionEntry` at :2549 uses `onConflict ... doUpdateSet`).

**Failure scenarios**:
1. User migrates, then resets session key K: reset mints a new sessionId and deletes old rows (`resetSqliteSessionEntryLifecycle`, `session-accessor.sqlite.ts:692-731`). `sessions.json` still maps K to the old sessionId → guard misses → every restart overwrites the live entry with the stale legacy snapshot; K points at a sessionId whose transcript rows were deleted. Post-reset conversation orphaned on every restart, forever.
2. Sessions deleted by user or pruned by maintenance are resurrected each boot (prune → re-import → prune cycle with `transcript_missing` warnings).
3. Downgrade→re-upgrade: a downgraded build resumes writing `sessions.json`; re-upgrade re-imports stale entries over newer SQLite rows.
4. Perf: for the common non-diverged case, `markAlreadyMigratedTranscript` calls `loadSqliteTranscriptEventsSync(...).length` — loads and JSON-parses EVERY transcript event row of every legacy session just to count, every boot (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:662-668`, accessor :953-976); `appendActiveSqliteTranscriptFileIssues` re-lists all entries + stats a path per entry each boot (:711-767). O(entire store) synchronous work blocking every gateway startup, permanently.
5. Related symptom: plain `openclaw doctor` nags forever after a clean migration — `countLegacyTranscript` (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:353-376`) has no already-migrated guard, so every archived transcript reports `transcript_missing` and doctor always says "Run doctor --fix to migrate" (doctor agent F7).

**Fix shape**: after a validated, issue-free import, archive/rename `sessions.json` out of the active path (manifest-tracked move, same mechanism already used for transcripts, restorable by `doctor --session-sqlite restore`), making import one-shot per store. Alternatively/additionally add an updatedAt freshness guard to `importSqliteSessionRows` upserts so stale legacy entries can never overwrite newer SQLite rows. Use event-count via `COUNT(*)` (`readOnlySqliteTranscriptEventCount` exists) instead of full row loads for the already-migrated check while it remains.

**Test gaps**: neither unit tests nor the e2e harness covers "second startup after a post-migration reset" (harness restarts the gateway BEFORE its reset/delete phases, `scripts/e2e/sqlite-sessions-transcripts-flip-proof.ts:298-401`).

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (doctor-migration agent F2 + production agent F3; independently confirmed). Severity: BLOCKER.

**Defect**: `src/gateway/server-startup-session-migration.ts:34-37` warn-lists only `transcript_missing` and `unreferenced_jsonl_archive_failed`. Every other issue code throws out of gateway boot (`server-startup-plugins.ts:76-85`, no catch), and there is NO escape hatch — no skip env var, no config flag (grepped; none exists). Nothing is auto-repaired, so the failure repeats identically on every boot: launchd restarts → same throw → permanent crash loop until the operator hand-runs doctor or edits files.

**Blocking codes that represent routine, benign legacy damage**:
- `transcript_malformed`: a single crash-truncated trailing JSONL line — the natural artifact of the OLD append-based runtime after power loss/disk-full — fails `JSON.parse` in `countTranscriptEventsForPath` (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite-readers.ts:37-56`; also `fatal:true` UTF-8 decode) and the whole session is not imported at all (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:402-408`) — no parse-prefix salvage. Unreadable files (EACCES) are also classified `transcript_malformed`.
- `entry_invalid`: one garbage value in `sessions.json`.
- `store_unreadable` / `store_not_object`: corrupt legacy store.
- `transcript_archive_failed`: a permission error moving a file the runtime doesn't even need (import + validation already succeeded); auto-restore then undoes the archive and the next boot crash-loops identically.

The user-facing error message itself is good (issue lines, doctor command, restore counts, failure-report path), and the fail-closed behavior is tested as intended (`server-startup-session-migration.test.ts:165`) — but for a mass upgrade this converts pre-existing file blemishes into total outages.

**Fix shape** (any subset removes most risk):
1. Tolerate a truncated FINAL line in JSONL (the overwhelmingly common corruption) — import the parseable prefix, warn.
2. Quarantine-and-warn per session: import what's parseable, leave damaged sessions as archive-tier artifacts with a doctor issue, don't block boot.
3. Demote `transcript_archive_failed` to warn-tier when import+validation succeeded (archive is optional hygiene at that point).
4. Provide an explicit operator escape hatch (env/config) to boot with import skipped for failing targets.

**Dependency note**: same files as the one-shot import fix — land after it to avoid churn. — the gap is power-loss durability specifically.

**Secondary defects (same code)**:
-  (:2245-2276) does  + full-file content comparisons INSIDE the write lock, lengthening lock hold on every archiving delete.
- Exporting a huge session's JSONL inside the DB write txn holds the write lock against all other processes for the duration of the file I/O.

**Fix shape**: fsync file + directory before the row-delete commit (or split into: write+fsync archive → then a short delete txn); move the archive-dedupe directory scan outside the transaction. Keep the ordering invariant: durable export strictly before delete, re-export idempotent.
EOF
)
payload.title: Startup migration fail-closed on routine malformed legacy state = upgrade crash loop

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (doctor-migration agent F1 + production agent F1; both independently confirmed). Severity: BLOCKER.

**Defect**: The gateway runs `runDoctorSessionSqlite({mode:"import", allAgents:true})` on EVERY boot (`src/gateway/server-startup-session-migration.ts:65-70`); targets are selected purely by `fs.existsSync(target.storePath)` (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:174-182`). Nothing ever tombstones/renames/archives `sessions.json` after a validated import (doctor only moves `.jsonl` files, `doctor-session-sqlite.ts:811-862`). Post-flip the runtime writes only SQLite, so `sessions.json` is frozen at migration time — and re-imported forever.

The only re-import guard is `markAlreadyMigratedTranscript` (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:423-435, 649-668`), which requires (a) transcript file missing AND (b) exact sessionId equality. `importSqliteSessionRows` is a blind upsert with no freshness/updatedAt guard (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:1222-1269`; `writeSessionEntry` at :2549 uses `onConflict ... doUpdateSet`).

**Failure scenarios**:
1. User migrates, then resets session key K: reset mints a new sessionId and deletes old rows (`resetSqliteSessionEntryLifecycle`, `session-accessor.sqlite.ts:692-731`). `sessions.json` still maps K to the old sessionId → guard misses → every restart overwrites the live entry with the stale legacy snapshot; K points at a sessionId whose transcript rows were deleted. Post-reset conversation orphaned on every restart, forever.
2. Sessions deleted by user or pruned by maintenance are resurrected each boot (prune → re-import → prune cycle with `transcript_missing` warnings).
3. Downgrade→re-upgrade: a downgraded build resumes writing `sessions.json`; re-upgrade re-imports stale entries over newer SQLite rows.
4. Perf: for the common non-diverged case, `markAlreadyMigratedTranscript` calls `loadSqliteTranscriptEventsSync(...).length` — loads and JSON-parses EVERY transcript event row of every legacy session just to count, every boot (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:662-668`, accessor :953-976); `appendActiveSqliteTranscriptFileIssues` re-lists all entries + stats a path per entry each boot (:711-767). O(entire store) synchronous work blocking every gateway startup, permanently.
5. Related symptom: plain `openclaw doctor` nags forever after a clean migration — `countLegacyTranscript` (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:353-376`) has no already-migrated guard, so every archived transcript reports `transcript_missing` and doctor always says "Run doctor --fix to migrate" (doctor agent F7).

**Fix shape**: after a validated, issue-free import, archive/rename `sessions.json` out of the active path (manifest-tracked move, same mechanism already used for transcripts, restorable by `doctor --session-sqlite restore`), making import one-shot per store. Alternatively/additionally add an updatedAt freshness guard to `importSqliteSessionRows` upserts so stale legacy entries can never overwrite newer SQLite rows. Use event-count via `COUNT(*)` (`readOnlySqliteTranscriptEventCount` exists) instead of full row loads for the already-migrated check while it remains.

**Test gaps**: neither unit tests nor the e2e harness covers "second startup after a post-migration reset" (harness restarts the gateway BEFORE its reset/delete phases, `scripts/e2e/sqlite-sessions-transcripts-flip-proof.ts:298-401`).
