**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK/extensions agent F1). Severity: BLOCKER. Confirmed by code reading; not runtime-verified — a runtime repro/disproof test is part of this task.

**Defect**: Live SQLite transcripts now index under extension-less logical paths `sessions/<agentId>/<sessionId>` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts:409-411, 796-830`). The memory_search tool filters sessions-source hits through `filterMemorySearchHitsBySessionVisibility` (`extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts:585`). For non-QMD hits the filter falls back to `extractTranscriptIdentityFromSessionsMemoryHit(hit.path)` (`extensions/memory-core/src/session-search-visibility.ts:121`), which only understands `.jsonl`/`.md` basenames and returns `null` for the new extension-less path (`src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.ts:89-123` — UNCHANGED by the PR) → `continue` drops the hit.

Only the QMD backend attaches the artifact-identity carrier that bypasses the parser (`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts:1748`); the built-in `MemoryIndexManager` returns raw `row.path` with no identity attached (`manager-search.ts:220, 292, 420`).

**Failure scenario**: any agent on the default (non-QMD) memory backend calls `memory_search` after the flip — every hit sourced from a live session transcript silently vanishes from results; only archived `.jsonl` artifacts still surface. No error, no log.

**Test gap**: `session-search-visibility.ts` / its test are untouched by the PR; the flip-proof e2e has no memory_search coverage. Add a test that indexes a live SQLite session on the built-in backend and asserts memory_search returns the hit through the visibility filter.

**Fix shape**: either teach `extractTranscriptIdentityFromSessionsMemoryHit` (or a successor) the extension-less live-path shape, or make the built-in backend attach the same identity carrier QMD attaches so the filter never needs to parse paths. Coordinate with the sessions-source re-index key decision (separate issue) so hit identity and row keys are settled together.
payload.title: memory_search silently drops all live-session hits on the built-in memory backend

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F6 + production agent F9). Severity: major. Confirmed.

**Defect**: `writeSqliteTranscriptArchive` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:2284-2316`) uses `writeFileSync` + `renameSync` with NO fsync on the file or its directory, and it executes INSIDE the same `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction that deletes the transcript rows (delete/maintenance paths via `deleteSqliteSessionStateIfUnreferenced`, :2392-2416). With `synchronous=NORMAL` WAL, a power cut just after COMMIT can persist the row deletion while the archive file contents are lost — violating RFC 0007 retention rule 1: "The archive export must be durable before the rows are deleted. ... The reverse order is forbidden because it can lose data."

Crash-safety for ordinary process kills IS correct (rows survive if the txn doesn't commit; re-export is idempotent via content-matched dedupe `findMatchingSqliteTranscriptArchive:2245-2276` and atomic tmp+rename with collision retry) — the gap is power-loss durability specifically.

**Secondary defects (same code)**:
- `findMatchingSqliteTranscriptArchive` (:2245-2276) does `readdirSync` + full-file content comparisons INSIDE the write lock, lengthening lock hold on every archiving delete.
- Exporting a huge session's JSONL inside the DB write txn holds the write lock against all other processes for the duration of the file I/O.

**Fix shape**: fsync file + directory before the row-delete commit (or split into: write+fsync archive → then a short delete txn); move the archive-dedupe directory scan outside the transaction. Keep the ordering invariant: durable export strictly before delete, re-export idempotent.
payload.title: Archive export not power-loss durable; file I/O runs inside the row-delete write transaction

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F5 + runtime agent F2/F3; independently confirmed). Severity: major (RFC invariant violation + unbounded invisible growth).

**Defect 1 — reset crash window**: `resetSqliteSessionEntryLifecycle` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:710-724`) commits txn A (delete old entry rows + write new entry with a new sessionId), then runs `await params.afterEntryMutation?.()` OUTSIDE any transaction — for gateway reset that's ACP metadata writes and awaited plugin hooks (`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:1043-1084`, unguarded) — then txn B archives+deletes the old session's rows. Gateway's `buildNextEntry` (:962-1035) carries no `usageFamilySessionIds`, so after txn A the old sessionId is unreferenced. A crash or ANY exception from `afterEntryMutation` skips txn B: the old transcript rows are never exported, never deleted, and never reclaimed. Note the sibling delete path already does entry-delete + archive in ONE transaction (`deleteSqliteSessionEntryLifecycle`, :743-764) — reset should match.

**Defect 2 — no orphan reclaim, multiple producers**: nothing sweeps `sessions` rows with no referencing entry:
- `applySqliteSessionEntryMaintenance` only reclaims sessionIds harvested from entries it removes in that same call (:2074-2135).
- `cleanupSqliteOrphanLifecycleTranscriptState` (:2418-2469) is dreaming-marker-scoped.
- doctor sweeps unreferenced JSONL FILES only, not rows (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:215/257`); no orphan-ROW repair mode exists.
Producers of orphaned rows besides the reset window:
- `sessions.delete` with `deleteTranscript:false` (`src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:2297,2349`): `deleteSqliteSessionEntryLifecycle` skips BOTH archive and row deletion when `archiveTranscript` is false (:748-756). File world left a user-readable transcript file; now "keep transcript" leaves invisible dead rows no runtime path can ever read (resume starts fresh by design).
- Agent deletion: `purgeSqliteDeletedAgentSessionEntries` (:906-943) deletes entry rows only — the deleted agent's transcripts in a shared store (default-agent store configs, `cleanup-service.ts:668-688`) are neither exported nor deleted. RFC lists agent deletion as a retention trigger requiring export-then-delete.
- Lifecycle removals with `archiveRemovedTranscript !== true` (:806-820).

This violates the RFC retention invariant ("Transcript rows live exactly as long as a session entry references their session id") and its crash story ("the next maintenance sweep re-exports idempotently" — no sweep will ever visit these ids). The file era had `pruneUnreferencedSessionArtifacts` (`disk-budget.ts:449`); the SQLite era has no row analog.

**Fix shape**: (1) make reset single-transaction (or move `afterEntryMutation` after archive+delete); (2) add a generic "archive+delete sessions rows with no referencing entry" sweep to maintenance (and expose it as a doctor repair) — this also backstops every other producer. Decide the product semantics of `sessions.delete deleteTranscript:false` under SQLite (probably: always export archive, then delete rows).

**Dependency**: build on the durable-export fix (archive write fsync/ordering) since the sweep reuses `writeSqliteTranscriptArchive`.
payload.title: Reset orphans transcript rows across a transaction gap; no unreferenced-row sweep exists to reclaim orphans

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F4, runtime agent F1, production agent F2 — three independent confirmations). Severity: major (explicit RFC 0007 requirement, not implemented at all).

**RFC 0007, retention boundary rule 2**: "Budget enforcement currently measures transcript file sizes. After the flip it measures per-session transcript row bytes in the store, with the same eviction policy."

**Defect**: `src/config/sessions/disk-budget.ts` has ZERO diff lines in the PR — `enforceSessionDiskBudget` still measures sessions-dir files + serialized JSON store bytes (`disk-budget.ts:59-100, 136-197, 550-851`), and its remaining callers are the `sessions cleanup` dry-run preview (`cleanup-service.ts:427-444`, dryRun:true) and the file-backend `store-maintenance-operations.ts` (off the runtime path now). Runtime SQLite maintenance (`session-accessor.sqlite.ts:2043-2136`) enforces only `pruneAfterMs`/`maxEntries`. No `SUM(LENGTH(event_json))` or any row-bytes measurement exists anywhere in the tree (grepped).

**Failure scenario**: a user with `session.maintenance.maxDiskBytes` set gets zero budget enforcement over live transcript rows in `agents/<id>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`; the DB grows until idle-prune (30d) / entry cap (500) fires. Aggravation: reply rotation carries all prior session ids in `usageFamilySessionIds` (`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:687-695`), keeping old transcripts referenced/hot for the whole prune window instead of archived at rotation as the file backend did (#14869) — bytes accumulate per conversation with nothing bounding them. Also `sessions cleanup` reports a `diskBudget` summary computed against files only — misleading operator output. Default is off, so it hits only users who opted in — but it is a silent contract regression for exactly the users who cared enough to configure it.

**Fix shape**: measure per-session `SUM(LENGTH(event_json))` (plus identities overhead if desired) in the store; drive the existing eviction policy (oldest-first eviction at the same trigger points) through the SQLite maintenance path, exporting-then-deleting via the same archive machinery; fix the `sessions cleanup` summary to report store row bytes. Consider an index-friendly incremental accounting if a full aggregate per sweep is too costly (tie in with hot-path perf work).

**Dependency**: reuse the unreferenced-row sweep / durable-export machinery (see reset-orphan issue) rather than adding a second delete path.
payload.title: Implement RFC disk-budget enforcement over per-session transcript row bytes

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F1; corroborated by runtime agent F5). Severity: major perf, compounding per message.

**Defect**: `readSessionEntryRow` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:1865-1894`) runs `selectFrom("session_entries").selectAll()` with NO WHERE clause, parses every `entry_json` blob, and builds a full in-memory store just to run `resolveSessionStoreEntry` alias resolution (`src/config/sessions/store-entry.ts:122-215`, whose final loop iterates all keys).

**Where it hurts** (all hot):
- `loadSqliteSessionEntry` — every gateway message-routing read.
- `patchSqliteSessionEntry` — called TWICE per patch, outside and inside the write txn (:355-375).
- `appendSqliteExpectedSessionTranscriptTurn` — twice per guarded turn append (:1343, :1363).
- `applySqliteSessionEntryMaintenance` (:2062-2072) adds another unconditional full-table load+parse on every entry write (load happens before the prune gates; `pruneStaleEntries` runs unconditionally).

With the default `maxEntries` cap of 500 and multi-KB entries (compaction checkpoints are embedded in `entry_json`), every message turn parses megabytes of JSON several times, INSIDE `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` write transactions, on the blocked synchronous event loop. Unlike the old file store there is no serialized-store cache. Every query also recompiles + re-prepares (`src/infra/kysely-sync.ts:39,65`).

**Failure scenario**: a busy multi-channel agent at the entry cap sees per-message latency grow linearly with store size, and every writer holds the DB write lock for the parse duration (which also widens the 30s busy-wait contention window for other processes).

**Fix shape**: alias resolution only needs the exact key, trimmed key, folded candidates, and keys whose normalized form equals the target — a bounded `WHERE session_key IN (...)` plus a keys-only scan; parsing all blobs is not required. Gate maintenance's full load behind the prune-interval check. Consider prepared-statement reuse in kysely-sync.
payload.title: Hot path: every keyed session-entry read loads and JSON-parses the entire session_entries table

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F2 + runtime agent F5; independently confirmed). Severity: major perf/policy. The helper predates the PR, but the PR routes the per-message session/transcript hot path through it, which is what makes it a defect now.

**Defect**: `openOpenClawAgentDatabase` (`src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts:543-551`) calls `registerAgentDatabase` on the CACHE-HIT path; `registerAgentDatabase` (:496-533) does a `statSync` of the DB file plus a `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` upsert into `agent_databases` in the SHARED `state/openclaw.sqlite` (`runOpenClawStateWriteTransaction`), updating `last_seen_at`/`size_bytes`. Every accessor call opens the DB; `runOpenClawAgentWriteTransaction` (:592) opens again — so one message append performs at least two global-state-DB write transactions. Additionally `ensureOpenClawAgentDatabasePermissions` (:594) runs after EVERY write transaction — `existsSync` + `chmodSync` across dir + up to 3 DB files (db/-wal/-shm).

**Consequences**:
1. All agents' hot-path operations serialize on the single global state-DB write lock — across processes (gateway, CLI, doctor, cron).
2. Hot-path `statSync` freshness polling, which root AGENTS.md explicitly bans ("Runtime hot paths: no freshness polling (stat/realpath/JSON reread/hash)").
3. Doubled WAL fsync traffic per append (agent DB + state DB).
4. Nothing even reads the registry's `size_bytes` for any status/doctor output today (observability issue tracks that separately).

**Fix shape**: registration is process-stable metadata — register once per process per path (first open), not per query; move `ensure...Permissions` to open-time; drop the per-open statSync (update size lazily/periodically or at close). Verify cross-process registry freshness expectations (which consumers need `last_seen_at` accuracy?) before choosing the cadence.
payload.title: Hot path: every agent-DB open (incl. cache hits) statSyncs the file and write-transacts the shared global state DB

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F3; runtime agent F5 confirmed the runner path mitigations). Severity: major perf on gateway-inject appends.

**Defect 1 — parentId full scan**: `appendSqliteTranscriptMessageInTransaction` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:1526-1529`) calls `readActiveTranscriptAppendParentId` (:3492-3499) whenever `options.parentId === undefined` — the normal case for gateway-injected messages (`src/gateway/server-methods/chat-transcript-inject.ts:204-215` sets no parentId) and user-turn persists (`src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.ts` passes none). That helper loads ALL `transcript_events` rows for the session, JSON.parses each, and runs `scanSessionTranscriptTree` — inside the write transaction, holding the write lock, on the sync event loop. A 10k-event session pays a full parse of 10k JSON blobs per appended message.

Mitigating context (verified): the embedded runner's per-message persistence passes an explicit parentId (`src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts:2288-2313`), so the scan fires mainly for gateway injects / user-turn persists — the asymptotics vs old JSONL leaf scans aren't strictly worse, but the constant factors (JSON parse of every row under the write lock) are.

**Defect 2 — compaction rewrite fan-out**: `replaceSqliteTranscriptEventsInTransaction` (:3501-3510): each re-appended event does `ensureTranscriptSessionRoot` (2 queries), identity lookups, and a `MAX(seq)` query — ~5n queries for an n-event compaction rewrite in one transaction.

**RFC context**: the hot-store promise ("resume, append, compaction ... reads and writes only these rows") is met; the gap is that appends are not O(1).

**Fix shape**: carry the append cursor (leaf/appendParentId) as a prepared fact on the session entry or a small indexed column (e.g. latest leaf per session in `transcript_event_identities` or a `sessions`-root column), or derive it via an indexed query instead of full-scan+tree-walk; batch/hoist the per-event queries in `replaceSqliteTranscriptEventsInTransaction` (resolve root once, allocate seq range once, bulk-insert identities).
payload.title: Hot path: transcript appends without explicit parentId are O(transcript size); compaction rewrite is ~5n queries

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (production agent F4). Severity: major perf for memory-enabled power users.

**Defect**: for SQLite-backed sessions, `buildSessionEntry` loads ALL transcript rows and serializes them just to derive size/freshness metadata (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts:791-812`, synchronous on the event loop), where the file branch used a stat call. Memory startup catchup iterates the full corpus (`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts` — sqlite corpus entries route through `buildSessionEntry`).

**Failure scenario**: a power user (hundreds of live sessions, 100k+ transcript rows) with memory enabled pays a full hot-store read+parse+serialize on every memory startup/catchup cycle — event-loop-blocking, repeated, and growing with store size.

**Fix shape**: derive freshness/size from cheap indexed aggregates — e.g. `MAX(seq)`/`MAX(rowid)`/entry `updatedAt` for freshness and `SUM(LENGTH(event_json))` for size (coordinate with the disk-budget row-bytes work, which needs the same aggregate) — and only read full rows for sessions whose freshness marker actually advanced.
payload.title: Memory startup catchup full-reads every hot SQLite transcript where the file era used fs.stat

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (runtime agent F4). Severity: major (AN 0002 violation + silent loss of shipped replay behavior).

**Defect**: `resetReplyRunSession` (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts:86-91`, called from `agent-runner.ts:1678` for role-ordering-conflict recovery) sets the new active entry's `sessionFile` to a real `<sessionId>.jsonl` path via `resolveSessionTranscriptPath` — AN 0002 requires a SQLite marker for the new active row after reset (gateway reset does this correctly via `formatSqliteSessionFileMarker`, `session-reset-service.ts:956-960`).

It then calls `persistSessionResetLifecycle` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts:1796-1800`) → `replayRecentUserAssistantMessages` (`src/config/sessions/transcript-replay.ts:61,66`), which runs `fs.existsSync`/`readFile` on `previousEntry.sessionFile` — at runtime a `sqlite:` marker — so it ALWAYS returns 0: the shipped DM-continuity tail replay silently no-ops on every SQLite reset. Worse, if the source ever were a stale real path, `transcript-replay.ts:92-106` would create a NEW active JSONL transcript on the runtime path (forbidden post-flip: only archive export may write files), and the replayed messages would never reach SQLite either way. The `cleanupTranscripts` branch archives via the file-move helper (`src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts:391-441`) — a no-op for SQLite rows.

**Mitigating (verified)**: no row leak — usage-family carry at `agent-runner-session-reset.ts:63-65` keeps old rows referenced; and the wrong `sessionFile` self-heals on the next turn append (`touchTranscriptTurnSessionEntry`). The real regressions are the dead replay and the marker violation.

**Fix shape**: build the next identity with `formatSqliteSessionFileMarker`; implement replay through the accessor (read recent rows from the previous sessionId via `readSessionTranscriptEvents`-equivalent, append via `appendTranscriptMessage`) instead of file reads/writes; route cleanup through the SQLite lifecycle (archive rows) rather than the file-move helper. Add a test: role-ordering-conflict reset on a SQLite session replays the recent user/assistant tail into the new session's rows.
payload.title: Auto-reply failure reset writes JSONL identity on the new active row; DM-continuity replay silently no-ops on SQLite

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F7). Severity: major design gap; exposure = any concurrent CLI/doctor/cron writer against a live gateway.

**Defect**: `patchSqliteSessionEntry` computes the patch from an entry read OUTSIDE the write transaction, then merges that possibly-stale patch onto the fresh row inside the transaction (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:353-396`). In-process writers are serialized by `SQLITE_SESSION_WRITER_QUEUES` (:238, `runQueuedStoreWrite`), but nothing serializes across processes; `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` only makes the final merge atomic, not the patch computation. A patch embedding stale-derived state (e.g. a rebuilt `compactionCheckpoints` array, accumulated token totals) silently drops a concurrent process's update.

**Same class**:
- `withSqliteTranscriptWriteLock` exposes `readEvents` → compute → `replaceEvents` across separate transactions (:1473-1496).
- `applySqliteSessionEntryLifecycleMutation` mutates a pre-transaction store snapshot (:792-876).

**Processes that write these DBs today**: gateway daemon, CLI one-shots, doctor, cron-in-gateway. The cross-process file write lock (`src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`) covers some paths but not these accessor seams.

**Fix shape (decision needed)**: either (a) declare and enforce "gateway is the only steady-state writer" — make CLI/doctor mutations route via gateway RPC or take the cross-process write lock at these seams, and state the invariant in code comments at the seams; or (b) make patches compute inside the write transaction (re-read + apply patch callback within txn — the sync-transaction design already re-reads fresh rows, so extend that to the patch-derivation inputs); or (c) add optimistic concurrency (updatedAt/version check, retry on conflict). Whichever stance is chosen, the transcript read→replace seam and lifecycle mutation snapshot need the same treatment.
payload.title: Cross-process read-modify-write races on entry_json: define and enforce writer ownership

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F2/F3 + SQLite-core agent F10 + runtime agent F6). Severity: major — this is AN 0002's "bridge becomes permanent architecture" failure mode in progress, plus a silent-breakage surface for external plugins.

**Defect 1 — silent public breakage**: `PluginCommandContext.sessionFile` now carries `sqlite:` markers while its doc still reads "Transcript file for the active OpenClaw session when available" (`src/plugins/types.ts:1988`; ditto `PluginCommandDiagnosticsSession.sessionFile` at :1952). Producers: `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.ts:47`, and `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts:222-249` literally hand-builds `sqlite:${agentId}:${sessionId}:${storePath}`. An external plugin command handler built pre-flip doing `fs.readFile(ctx.sessionFile)` or `path.dirname(...)` gets a marker string — ENOENT/garbage at runtime with no compile error, no deprecation notice, no docs. This is exactly the silent-mutation mode RFC 0007's removal-not-stub principle was meant to prevent. Other marker-emitting plugin-visible surfaces: `SessionEntry.sessionFile` via `getSessionEntry`/`listSessionEntries`, and `EmbeddedAgentRunResult` meta (active-memory hand-parses it, proving bundled code already needs discrimination).

**Defect 2 — 7+ duplicate marker implementations, none public**: canonical helpers live in `src/config/sessions/sqlite-marker.ts`, but the only SDK export is `formatSqliteSessionFileMarker` via `src/plugin-sdk/sqlite-runtime-testing.ts:18` — a subpath headed "Private local-only SQLite lifecycle helpers for first-party tests". Meanwhile bundled-plugin PROD code re-implements the regex: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.ts:109`, `trajectory.ts:146`, `startup-binding.ts:178`, `session-binding.ts:44`, `extensions/active-memory/index.ts:1740`, a prefix check in `extensions/feishu/src/doctor.ts:126`, and an inline FORMATTER in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts:227`. `packages/memory-host-sdk` deep-imports the core helper. Any change to the marker shape (e.g. AN 0002's planned typed-target refactor) breaks four plugins silently.

**Defect 3 — divergent private formatter can emit unparseable markers**: `formatSqliteTranscriptTarget` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:3666-3669`) omits the store-path segment when `scope.path` is unset, producing `sqlite:<agent>:<session>` — which `parseSqliteSessionFileMarker` (`sqlite-marker.ts:10,18-34`) rejects (regex requires three segments). An entry persisted with that variant would be treated as a FILE PATH by every marker-discriminating consumer. All current callers pass storePath (verified), but `SqliteBranchCheckpointSessionParams.storePath` / `SqliteSessionImportRowsParams.storePath` are optional — one caller away from the failure. It also skips the `path.resolve` the canonical formatter applies. Delete it; use the canonical helper.

**Defect 4 — small marker-discipline miss**: `prewarmSessionFile` does a guaranteed-ENOENT `fs.open("sqlite:...")` per embedded run (`run/attempt.ts:2340` → `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-cache.ts:70`); gate on the marker or drop.

**Fix shape**: (1) export ONE transitional marker-discrimination helper from a real (documented-as-transitional) SDK subpath, consolidate all seven copies onto it; (2) fix `PluginCommandContext.sessionFile`/diagnostics docs + add `@deprecated` with the typed replacement (or replace with a typed target field now); (3) delete `formatSqliteTranscriptTarget` in favor of `formatSqliteSessionFileMarker`; (4) fix prewarm. Longer term this feeds AN 0002's typed-target refactor — but the bridge needs an owner NOW.
payload.title: Marker bridge leaking into public SDK: fix PluginCommandContext.sessionFile contract; consolidate 7+ marker impls behind one supported helper

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F4). Severity: major (the RFC's own stage-3 obligations for the flip).

**Defect**: RFC 0007's deprecation plan stage 3 requires every deprecated surface to be visible in (1) TS `@deprecated` comments, (2) plugin SDK docs, (3) `openclaw plugins inspect --runtime` compatibility notices, (4) external plugin-inspector advisory rules — BEFORE the flip removes them.

Verified state at PR head:
- TS `@deprecated` tags and docs existed pre-flip (visible as deletions in the diff) — TypeScript authors got warnings. OK.
- Inspector: NO session/transcript deprecation notice exists in `src/plugins/status.ts` or `src/cli/plugins-inspect-command.ts`, neither at merge-base nor at head (only `deprecated-memory-embedding-provider-api` exists). The requirement was never implemented anywhere in the window. JS-only plugin authors had exactly zero discoverable notice before removal.
- `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md` received only a one-line table tweak — no flip-removal migration section mapping each removed API (loadSessionStore/saveSessionStore/updateSessionStore, resolveSessionFilePath, resolveSessionTranscriptPathInDir, resolveAndPersistSessionFile, readLatestAssistantTextFromSessionTranscript, SessionTranscriptUpdate.sessionFile, sync sessionFiles, transcriptPath/sessionFile params) to its replacement.
- The `chore: refresh plugin sdk api baseline after rebase` commit absorbed all removals in one `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` hash bump. (What it absorbed is legitimate — surface budgets in `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs:112-215` were ratcheted DOWN, no hidden growth — but the process step it papered over is this issue.)

**Fix shape**: add post-flip inspector detection for removed/marker-affected surfaces (a plugin importing a removed subpath export, or reading `sessionFile`-shaped fields, should get a compatibility notice naming the replacement); write the sdk-migration.md removal table (old API → replacement → removal boundary); update plugin-inspector advisory rules. Since the flip removes rather than deprecates, the notices should be phrased as removal/migration guidance. Coordinate with the marker-bridge issue for which surfaces remain transitional.
payload.title: RFC deprecation-window obligations unmet: no inspector notices ever shipped; sdk-migration docs lack flip-removal section

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F5). Severity: major (duplicate dreaming LLM batches/notes = wasted spend + duplicate memory artifacts).

**Defect**: dreaming's seen-message hashes embed a session scope key (`hashSessionMessageId(`${sessionScope}\n...`)`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts:1034`). The PR gives the two storage tiers DIFFERENT scope shapes:
- SQLite live sessions: `${agentId}:sessions/<agentId>/<sessionId>` (`dreaming-phases.ts:990-993`);
- file/archive artifacts: `buildSessionScopeKey` → `${agentId}:<sessionId>` (`dreaming-phases.ts:686-690`; `parseUsageCountedSessionIdFromFileName` strips `.jsonl[.reset|.deleted.*]`, `src/config/sessions/artifacts.ts:123-135`).

Pre-flip, a live file and its rotated reset/deleted archives shared one scope, so seen-hashes carried across rotation. Post-flip:
1. First run after migration: every live session gets a fresh scope → all previously-dreamed messages inside the lookback window are re-ingested (duplicate dreaming batches/notes) — a one-time storm across the whole live working set.
2. Every tier move (session reset or delete archives RECENT rows immediately): the archive is scanned under the old-style scope with an empty seen-set and re-dreams the same messages — an ongoing, per-reset duplication.

Cursor state keys changed shape the same way (`buildSessionStateKey`, `dreaming-phases.ts:722-724`).

**Fix shape**: derive ONE scope from session identity (`${agentId}:<sessionId>`) for both tiers — the live-scope construction should stop embedding the logical corpus path. Provide a doctor/state migration for existing seen-hash/cursor state if the chosen canonical shape differs from what pre-flip file scopes produced, or accept and document the one-time storm explicitly.
payload.title: Dreaming ingestion dedupe breaks across migration boundary and live→archive tier moves (re-dream storms)

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F6). Severity: major product judgment — needs a decision (mitigate or explicitly accept), not necessarily code.

**Defect**: memory row keys for live transcripts changed shape from `sessions/<agent>/<sessionId>.jsonl` to `sessions/<agent>/<sessionId>`. On the first source-wide sync after the flip, `deleteStaleRows` removes every old-keyed row (`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts:2214-2245`) and every live session re-embeds from scratch — the hash short-circuit is keyed by `entry.path`, so it can never match across the rename. Bounded by the live working set (≤ maxEntries, default 500 transcripts), but that is a nontrivial one-time embedding cost — real API money for API-embedding users — with no key-migration in doctor and no acknowledgment anywhere in the PR/docs.

**Secondary**: the same tier-key mismatch means a pruned session's content can transiently exist under BOTH the stale live-keyed doc and the new archive-keyed doc until the next full sync (targeted syncs pass `existingRows: null`, skipping stale deletion — `manager-sync-ops.ts:2184-2189`), so memory_search can briefly double-report. Parity with pre-flip is only partial: the stale live doc no longer has a file whose deletion is noticed cheaply.

**Fix shape (pick one)**: (a) doctor key-migration that renames existing `sessions/.../<id>.jsonl` rows to the new key shape (preserving embeddings, avoiding the storm); or (b) explicitly accept the one-time re-embed — state it in the PR body/release notes and make targeted syncs clean up stale cross-tier duplicates. Coordinate with the memory_search visibility fix so hit-identity parsing and row-key shape are settled together (that fix may constrain the key shape).
payload.title: Memory sessions-source full re-index storm on first post-flip sync + transient cross-tier double-reporting

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (production agent F5). Severity: major production gap ("missing entirely").

**Defect**: with sessions/transcripts flipped into `agents/<id>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`, DB corruption (power loss, kernel panic, cloud-sync interference) now takes out the gateway — and there is no story:
- No `PRAGMA integrity_check`/`quick_check` anywhere in product code (only `scripts/bench-sqlite-state.ts:448`).
- SQLITE_CORRUPT during startup import propagates as a raw throw out of `inspectOrMigrateTarget` → gateway boot failure with no guidance.
- doctor `recover`/`restore` handle migration-run manifests only (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite-recover-report.ts`) — no move-aside/rebuild/`.recover` path for a corrupt DB.

**What exists (verified)**: doctor warns on cloud-synced state dirs (`docs/gateway/doctor.md:400`); network-FS safety downgrades/refusals (`src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts:165-253`); backups snapshot via `VACUUM INTO` with busy timeout and refuse un-snapshotable DBs (`src/infra/backup-create.ts:645-690`). Those reduce the odds; none help once corruption happens.

**Failure scenario**: power loss corrupts the agent DB → permanent crash loop; user has no doctor command that names the problem or repairs it; manual surgery required.

**Fix shape**: (1) classify SQLITE_CORRUPT/NOTADB at startup and emit a specific, actionable error naming the DB path and the doctor command; (2) add a doctor repair mode: integrity_check → attempt `.recover`-style salvage into a fresh DB (or `VACUUM INTO`) → on failure, move the corrupt DB aside (preserving it for support) and start fresh, with explicit operator confirmation for the destructive step; (3) optionally a lightweight `quick_check` in `doctor --session-sqlite inspect`.
payload.title: No corruption story for the now-load-bearing agent DB (SQLITE_CORRUPT = unexplained crash loop)
Flip-proof e2e never runs in per-PR CI; close proof coverage gaps (crash-mid-migration, SQLITE_BUSY, scale, downgrade cycle, corrupt DB, Bun)

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (doctor-migration agent F3). Severity: major (privacy; AN 0005 sanitization requirement).

**Defect**: `createSessionSqliteMigrationFailureIssue` (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite-migration-run.ts:264-309`) drops the structured `sessionKey` FIELD but keeps `issue.message` verbatim, sanitized only by `sanitizeFailureReportText` (:539-545), which redacts emails and `token=`-style patterns. Issue messages embed session keys — e.g. "SQLite entry is missing for ${normalizedKey}" (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:463`) and the `sqlite_entry_mismatch`/count-mismatch messages — and session keys can carry channel chat ids / phone numbers. Messages also embed full absolute paths ("${target.storePath}: ...", "Transcript file is missing: /Users/<name>/..."); `shortenFailureReportPath` is applied to structured path fields but never inside messages.

AN 0005 requires: shortened paths, no transcript contents, no unredacted personal/environment data in support artifacts and issue bodies.

**Test gap**: the existing test only proves redaction for a crafted `token=` string and a session key that never appears in any message.

**Fix shape**: build issue messages from structured parts and sanitize at composition time (redact/shorten sessionKey and paths in the message templates), or run message-level sanitization that (a) applies `shortenFailureReportPath` to any absolute path substring and (b) hashes/elides session keys. Add tests asserting a phone-number-shaped session key and a home-dir path never appear in `.failure.md`, `.failure.json`, or the generated issue body/URL.
payload.title: Migration-failure GitHub issue bodies leak session keys and full home paths through issue messages

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review — CI check `checks-node-compact-small-2` failing at PR head ba1b55173a5 (run 28550110657, job 84645196676).

**Failure**: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts` > "runCronIsolatedAgentTurn message tool policy > keeps shared cron run context references active after completion" — `AssertionError: expected undefined to match object { Object (sessionKey) }`.

A cron isolated-agent run's shared run-context is expected to retain a session reference (sessionKey) after completion; post-flip it comes back undefined. This is squarely in flipped territory (cron session state now lives in SQLite rows; the runtime review verified `src/cron/.../run-session-state.ts:31-37` reads via the accessor) — likely the completion path no longer projects the session entry (or the entry lookup misses under the new accessor) rather than a test-only issue. Diagnose whether the product behavior regressed (cron run context losing its session binding = downstream consumers can't resolve the session after a run) or the test needs a legitimate update for changed semantics; fix accordingly. Do not silence the test without proving current behavior is the intended contract.
payload.title: CI red at PR head: cron isolated-agent message-tool-policy test — session context sessionKey undefined after completion

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (doctor-migration agent F4, F5, F6, F8, F9, F12-part). Severity: major-to-minor bundle over the same code; grouped because they reshape the same doctor/migration-run surfaces. Land after the one-shot-import and startup-policy fixes (same files; one-shot import also eliminates most per-boot growth).

1. **Manifest growth (F4, major pre-one-shot)**: `runDoctorSessionSqlite` writes a manifest for EVERY import run (`src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.ts:103-106`) and startup currently runs import every boot → one file per boot forever in `state/session-sqlite-migration-runs/` (plus `.failure.json`/`.failure.md` pairs per failing boot in a crash loop). No pruning exists (grepped repo-wide). Add retention (e.g. keep last N + all failed), and skip manifest creation for no-op runs.

2. **Archive gating disabled by one warn-tier issue (F5)**: archive runs only when `report.issues.length === 0` (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:237`) — but `transcript_missing` is warn-tier and common. One missing sidecar means the target never archives anything: every subsequent run re-reads and JSON.parses every line of every transcript (three full passes per file: count at :383, reader insert at :416, validate count at :476), and `importSqliteSessionRows` loads the entire existing event set as JSON strings into a Set for dedupe (`session-accessor.sqlite.ts:1247-1250`, `readTranscriptEventJsonSetInTransaction` :977-987). Gate archive on blocking issues only (or per-record), and use `COUNT(*)` (`readOnlySqliteTranscriptEventCount` exists) instead of materializing event arrays in `validateImportedRecordBeforeArchive` (:488-494).

3. **Crash mid-run invisible to recover (F6)**: `isFailedSessionSqliteMigrationManifest` (`doctor-session-sqlite-migration-run.ts:465-471`) requires `failedAt`/`failureReports`/recorded issues. A process killed mid-import leaves none of these → `doctor --session-sqlite recover` says "No failed migration manifest found" for exactly the run it should recover (`restore` all-manifests still works). Treat startedAt-without-completedAt as failed/incomplete. Also: per-record import exceptions (disk-full, SQLite errors) are uncaught (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:388,410`) — the run aborts with a raw error and no failure report; wrap and report.

4. **Report attribution (F8, minor)**: `restore-report.ts:27-39` and `recover-report.ts:55-63` attach the combined restore report and all `restore_conflict` issues to `targetReports[0]` — multi-agent restores lose per-agent attribution.

5. **Stale planned moves from the EEXIST retry loop (F9, minor/rare)**: `moveSessionJsonlToArchive` records a planned move before EACH rename attempt (`doctor-session-sqlite.ts:830-860`); a lost EEXIST attempt followed by a suffixed success leaves a dead planned pair that restore later reports as "source and archive are both missing" (`migration-run.ts:396-400`). Record planned move once with the final chosen path, or mark superseded attempts.

6. **Prefilled issue URL length (F12-part, minor)**: the no-gh fallback embeds up to a 20KB body in the URL query string (`migration-run.ts:479-485`), exceeding GitHub/browser limits (~8KB) — the link can 414/truncate. Truncate the URL body variant with a pointer to the local `.failure.md`.
payload.title: Doctor migration hygiene: manifest growth, archive gating on warn-tier issues, crash-run recover visibility, report attribution, minor restore edge cases

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (doctor-migration agent F11; AN 0005 explicit requirement). Severity: minor docs gap, upgrade-risk relevant.

**Defect**: AN 0005 requires downgrade instructions telling operators to restore archived artifacts before launching a previous file-backed OpenClaw version. `docs/cli/doctor.md` documents restore mechanics, but NO doc mentions downgrade at all (grepped doctor.md, session-management-compaction.md, session.md, faq.md).

**Content needed**: after the flip, transcripts referenced by live entries were renamed into `session-sqlite-import-archive/`; an older runtime looks for them at original `sessionFile` paths and for `sessions.json` (left in place but going stale — note interaction with the one-shot-import fix if it starts renaming sessions.json: the downgrade doc must then say restore brings it back). Document: (1) `openclaw doctor --session-sqlite restore --session-sqlite-all-agents` before downgrading; (2) what state is and is not recovered (sessions created post-flip exist only in SQLite and will not appear to the old runtime); (3) the re-upgrade behavior (and its safety once the freshness-guard/one-shot fix lands). Place in doctor docs + the session-management reference; link from any release/upgrade notes for the flip release.
payload.title: Document the downgrade procedure (restore archived artifacts before running an older file-backed version)

payload.description:
**Source**: ClawSweeper P2 review finding on PR #98236, independently re-verified at PR head ba1b55173a5 during the 2026-07-01 review. Severity: P2 regression vs main.

**Defect**: overflow recovery now unconditionally calls `truncateOversizedToolResultsInRuntimeTranscript` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts:2994` and `:3066`), whose reader `readRuntimeTranscriptFileState` throws "runtime transcript target is not SQLite-backed" for any non-marker `sessionFile` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-truncation.ts:75-82`). The throw is swallowed by the catch at `tool-result-truncation.ts:1160-1164`, returning `{truncated:false, reason}` — so recovery logs "truncation did not help" and gives up.

**Reachability**: `runEmbeddedAgent` still accepts a caller-supplied `sessionFile` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts:21`, honored via `src/agents/run-session-target.ts:51-58`, exposed through plugin runtime `types-core.ts`), so explicit file-backed targets remain reachable in embedded runs. On current main those sessions can truncate oversized tool results during overflow recovery; on the PR they silently cannot — the session wedges on context overflow instead of recovering.

**Fix shape** (ClawSweeper's suggested repair, agreed): branch on the target — when `activeSessionFile` is not a SQLite marker, call the file-backed `truncateOversizedToolResultsInSession` (`tool-result-truncation.ts:1170`); keep the runtime-transcript helper for markers. Add a test with an explicit file `sessionFile` reaching overflow recovery. Alternative worth considering: if explicit-file embedded runs are intended to die at the flip, remove the param instead — but that is a separate compat decision (RFC lists `runEmbeddedAgent({sessionFile})` as deprecated-then-removed, and it is currently only deprecated).
payload.title: Overflow recovery cannot truncate oversized tool results for explicit non-SQLite sessionFile targets

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F9, F11, F12, F13; production agent F10). Severity: minor bundle.

1. **Redundant index on the hottest table (F9)**: `idx_agent_transcript_events_session ON transcript_events(session_id, seq)` (`src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.sql:126-127`) exactly duplicates the composite PRIMARY KEY's implicit index (:122). Every append maintains both. Drop it (requires schema-baseline regen — the drift guard will correctly force an intentional commit; coordinate a user_version bump/migration for existing DBs).

2. **`sessions.status` CHECK vs unvalidated legacy JSON (F11)**: `bindSqliteSessionRoot` passes any non-empty string as `status` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:2669`) into the CHECK-constrained column (`schema.sql:19`), while `chat_type` IS whitelisted (`normalizeSqliteChatType`). Doctor import parses legacy `sessions.json` entries without validating status (`parseSessionEntryRow` is a blind cast) — a legacy `status: "cancelled"` makes `writeSessionEntry` throw an opaque SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_CHECK and abort the import transaction (which, pre-startup-policy-fix, bricks boot). Normalize status against the closed set like chat_type.

3. **No VACUUM/reclaim story (F12)**: deletes of large transcripts free pages internally but never shrink the file; the only VACUUM in-tree is backup's `VACUUM INTO` (`src/infra/backup-create.ts:542/667`). WAL checkpointing is fine (autocheckpoint 1000 pages, 30-min PASSIVE, TRUNCATE on close — `src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts:10-16, 299-336`). Combined with the missing row-bytes disk budget, heavy users see monotonic file growth with no reclaim and no doctor repair. Options: periodic incremental_vacuum (requires auto_vacuum pragma set before schema creation — migration decision), or a doctor compact command.

4. **30s synchronous busy-wait can freeze the gateway event loop (F13/prod F10)**: `busy_timeout = 30_000` (`src/state/openclaw-state-db.ts:36`) with sync node:sqlite blocks the calling thread — in the gateway, the entire event loop — for up to 30s per contention plus up to 8 commit retries (`src/infra/sqlite-transaction.ts:95`). Exhaustion yields a raw "database is locked" error. The hot-path fixes (bounded entry reads, once-per-process registration, O(1) appends, IO-out-of-txn) shrink the contention windows and should land first; then reassess whether 30s is the right ceiling for the gateway (vs a shorter timeout + async retry with logging).
payload.title: SQLite schema/hardening minors: redundant index, status CHECK vs legacy JSON, VACUUM/reclaim story, 30s sync busy-wait

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F7). Severity: minor (documented-SDK param changed meaning with no doc/type change).

**Defect**: the SDK passes `archiveRemovedEntryTranscripts` through (`src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.ts:230-238`), but `cleanupSqliteSessionLifecycleArtifacts` never reads it (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:660-689`); removed-entry transcript rows are ALWAYS exported to archive JSONL (`deleteSqliteSessionStateIfUnreferenced`, :2392-2416). Pre-flip, `false` skipped the archive step (see merge-base `src/config/sessions/store.ts:1539,1558`).

**Impact**: dreaming passes `false` (`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts:792-799`), so dreaming's scratch/subagent session transcripts — which the flag existed to discard quietly — now get exported as archive artifacts on every narrative cleanup, accumulating junk JSONL in the archive dir (which is also scanned by memory/QMD indexing and dreaming itself — feeds the scope-key duplication issue).

**RFC context**: RFC 0007 blesses `cleanupSessionLifecycleArtifacts` as the narrow dreaming cleanup surface; the SQLite implementation "may change how this is performed internally" but the contract semantics should hold.

**Fix shape**: honor the flag in the SQLite path (skip archive export, still delete rows) — or, if always-archive is the deliberate new semantics, remove the parameter from the SDK signature and update dreaming + docs in the same change (no silent no-op params).
payload.title: cleanupSessionLifecycleArtifacts({archiveRemovedEntryTranscripts}) silently ignored on SQLite — dreaming scratch now exported as archives

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F8). Severity: minor (RFC conformance / API surface decision).

**Defect**: RFC 0007 explicitly said the lifecycle-cleanup export must "not grow into a broader deletion or arbitrary cleanup API." The PR adds to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime`:
- `deleteSessionEntry` (`src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.ts:182-...`) — a general session deletion API;
- `resolveSessionStoreBackupPaths` (:~200-219) — backup path resolution (serves the feishu doctor's WAL/SHM-aware backup);
- `loadTranscriptEventsSync` (:128-137) — returns `unknown[]`, synchronous SQLite read exposed on a subpath used by channel hot paths.

They are tested (`session-store-runtime.test.ts:212-235`) but absent from `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`/`sdk-subpaths.md`, and were never RFC-sanctioned.

**Fix shape (decision)**: either (a) accept them as public API — document in sdk-runtime/sdk-subpaths, note them as an RFC amendment (they serve real bundled-plugin needs: feishu doctor repair, memory-host-sdk), and give `loadTranscriptEventsSync` a typed return; or (b) narrow: move them off the public subpath into injected runtime helpers / plugin-internal seams so the public deletion surface stays as the RFC specified. Either way the current state (undocumented public exports contradicting the RFC's stated boundary) should not persist.
payload.title: New SDK exports added at the flip (deleteSessionEntry, backup paths, sync transcript reads) contradict RFC narrowness: document or narrow

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F9). Severity: minor (public event contract narrowed silently + forbidden pattern).

**Defect 1**: emitters that publish `{ sessionFile: archivedPath }` only — archive/rotation events from `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:2388`, `store.ts:818`, and `src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts:371` — are now invisible to public `onSessionTranscriptUpdate` subscribers: the public listener drops target-less updates (`src/sessions/transcript-events.ts:62-65`). External plugins that watched archive rotations via the public listener stop hearing them with no type-level signal (the event type simply no longer admits what these emitters produce).

**Defect 2**: bundled memory-core is unaffected only because `src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation.ts:48-55` swaps in the INTERNAL listener via a `globalThis` symbol — an import-time global side effect the extensions guide forbids — and its comment ("public SDK listener stays file-backed during the compatibility window") is now false post-flip.

**Fix shape**: decide the public contract: (a) if archive events are public, give them a structured target variant (e.g. `targetKind: "archive-artifact"` with an explicit artifact path — note RFC wanted `targetKind` on the target anyway) and let the public listener carry them; or (b) if archive events are internal-only, type the internal emitter separately and remove the pretense — then replace the globalThis swap with a properly injected internal subscription, and delete/update the stale comment either way.
payload.title: Public transcript-update listeners silently lose archive events; memory-core works only via a globalThis side-channel with a stale contract comment

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F10). Severity: minor (repo-policy violation: legacy readers belong in doctor only; low blast radius).

**Defect**: `resolveSessionFileForSyncTarget` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts:483-536`) falls back to `readSessionTranscriptClassificationStore(path.join(sessionsDir, "sessions.json"))` when a sync target has no corpus match. Post-flip that file is migration debt (doctor imports it; it is not runtime-canonical — and after the one-shot-import fix it may be renamed away entirely). The branch either reads stale metadata (potentially resolving an old JSONL path for an already-migrated session) or noise-fails. Corpus match wins for anything live, so exposure is low — but it is exactly the "runtime read-through fallback to legacy sessions.json" that root AGENTS.md and AN 0005 forbid.

**Fix shape**: delete the fallback branch; a sync target with no corpus match should resolve to "not found" (targeted sync for an unknown session is a no-op/warn). Verify no test depends on the fallback and that archived-artifact sync targets still resolve through the corpus/archive tier. Coordinate with the one-shot-import change (which may remove the file this reads).
payload.title: Remove memory-host-sdk steady-state runtime fallback reader of legacy sessions.json

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SDK agent F11). Severity: minor.

**Defect**: `extensions/feishu/src/doctor.ts:352-356` returns `[]` candidates for marker-valued `sessionFile`, so the blank-user-message corruption scan only ever inspects legacy file-backed sessions. Post-flip every live session is SQLite-backed → the doctor's inspection arm never scans live state again. The rest of the feishu doctor SQLite wiring is correct (verified): repair of legacy files still works, and the new `resolveSessionStoreBackupPaths` / `deleteSessionEntry(archiveTranscript: true)` usage including WAL/SHM backup is sound.

**Fix shape (decision)**: if the blank-user-message corruption class is file-era-only (produced by the old JSONL writer), keep the skip but add the required inline comment stating the contract ("scan targets legacy file transcripts only; SQLite rows cannot exhibit this corruption because ..."). If the corruption can occur in SQLite rows (e.g. via imported legacy events), extend the scan to read rows via the accessor for marker-backed sessions.
payload.title: Feishu doctor blank-message transcript scan is permanently dark for SQLite sessions — implement or document scope

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (SQLite-core agent F8; matches AN 0004's residual risk). Severity: minor today (gateway is the sole injector), design-relevant for the writer-ownership decision.

**Defect**: the assistant-scoped duplicate check (`shouldAppend` → `findInjectedAssistantMessageByIdempotencyKey`, `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-transcript-inject.ts:204-215`) runs in `selectAppendableSqliteTranscriptTurnMessages` (`src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts:1350-1359, 1416-1428`) — inside the in-process writer queue but BEFORE the write transaction. Two PROCESSES injecting the same idempotency key can both pass the check; the second insert is not stopped because caller-checked appends deliberately omit duplicate keys from the unique `(session_id, message_idempotency_key)` index (:3433-3444, per AN 0004). Scan-mode dedupe is safe (its check runs inside BEGIN IMMEDIATE — verified).

**Also note (designed divergence, verify consumers)**: a later scan-mode lookup for a caller-checked key returns the INDEXED row (e.g. the user row), never the unindexed assistant row — any future consumer using the identity index as a general payload-key lookup will miss caller-checked rows.

**Fix shape**: depends on the cross-process writer-ownership stance (see that issue). If single-writer is enforced, document this as an invariant at the seam (inline comment per root AGENTS.md rules) and close. If multi-process writes remain legal, move the caller-supplied `shouldAppend` evaluation inside the write transaction (the accessor already re-reads inside txn for the session-rebound check) or take the cross-process write lock around check+append.
payload.title: Caller-checked transcript idempotency can double-append across processes (unique index deliberately bypassed)

payload.description:
**Source**: PR #98236 review (production agent F8). Severity: minor (present-but-weak; matters the first time something is slow in the field).

**Defect**: `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts` has ZERO logging — no logger import, no timing; failures surface as raw exceptions (a contended write manifests as a silent 30s event-loop stall followed by "database is locked"). `doctor --session-sqlite inspect` reports entry/event COUNTS but no DB file size, WAL size, or per-session row bytes. The `agent_databases` registry dutifully records `size_bytes` on every open (`src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts:496-533`) but nothing reads it for any status/doctor output.

**Fix shape**: (1) slow-op logging in the accessor (threshold-gated, e.g. warn when a write txn or entry read exceeds N ms, including sessionKey/op name — no per-op noise); (2) surface BUSY/LOCKED waits explicitly (count + duration) instead of silent stalls; (3) extend `doctor --session-sqlite inspect` with DB file size, WAL size, per-session row bytes (reuse the disk-budget aggregate), and top-N largest sessions; (4) make something read `agent_databases.size_bytes` (gateway status or doctor) or stop writing it per-open (coordinate with the once-per-process registration fix).
payload.title: Observability for the SQLite session hot path: timing/failure logging + doctor inspect DB/WAL/row-byte stats

payload.body: Likely already addressed on the PR branch after the review snapshot: the branch moved from ba1b55173a5 (reviewed) to 7e621c12bdc, adding fc3fd07f0aa 'fix: preserve shared cron run context ownership' and 3871ff9b3e0 'fix: align sqlite flip with latest cron cleanup' (both touch src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts + src/infra/agent-events.ts — the exact surface behind this failure). Before closing: confirm checks-node-compact-small-2 is green on 7e621c12bdc (or run 'node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts' at that head) — the review-captured failure was from run 28550110657 at the older head.

Note: review snapshot was PR head ba1b55173a5 (2026-07-01). The branch has since been rebased and advanced to 7e621c12bdc (net-new: two cron run-context fixes — see .23 comment — plus an i18n inventory refresh). All other findings were re-checked as rebase-stable in nature (file/line refs may drift slightly). Local main-repo branch now tracks the PR head exactly; pre-sync local tip preserved at backup/sqlite-flip-local-20260701.

Live production confirmation on Josh's gateway (2026-07-01 22:59:07Z boot, ~/.openclaw, flip build 7e621c12bdc): startup import ran with targets=47 legacyEntries=73 sqliteEntries=123 and importedEntries=8 importedTranscriptEvents=0 — i.e. 8 stale legacy entries are re-upserted from frozen sessions.json on EVERY gateway restart (plus a recurring [transcript_missing] warning for agent:main:cron:60723825... referencing a long-gone JSONL). This is the re-import loop in the wild: those 8 entries' sessionIds/transcripts no longer line up with live SQLite state, so the already-migrated guard misses them forever. Useful repro data: the 'main' agent store at ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json still carries the 73 legacy entries.

Worker 5 partial: added SQLite runtime maxDiskBytes/highWaterBytes enforcement using session entry JSON bytes plus transcript event_json row bytes, oldest-first eviction, archive/delete via existing SQLite lifecycle removal path, and conformance coverage in src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts. Focused proof: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts. Leaving open because sessions cleanup dry-run/apply summaries still call the file-backed enforceSessionDiskBudget helper, so operator diskBudget reporting is not yet store-row-byte aware.

Worker 5 partial: added SDK migration docs for removed session/transcript file APIs and OpenClaw plugins inspect compatibility notices for external plugins whose load errors/diagnostics reference removed session file surfaces; registered removed-session-transcript-file-api in the plugin compatibility registry with status removed. Focused proof: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/status.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts; docs: pnpm docs:check-mdx and pnpm format:docs:check. Leaving open because the issue also calls out external @openclaw/plugin-inspector advisory rules and dependency .13 remains open.

Worker 5 partial: wired the in-process SQLite sessions/transcripts flip proof into per-PR CI as check-sqlite-session-flip-proof / sqlite-session-flip-proof and validated the exact command locally: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts test/scripts/sqlite-sessions-transcripts-flip-proof.e2e.test.ts. Workflow sanity passed with pnpm check:workflows. Leaving open because crash-mid-migration, true SQLITE_BUSY, scale, downgrade/re-upgrade, corrupt DB, Bun, and second-startup-after-reset coverage gaps remain.

Worker 5: documented downgrade procedure in doctor docs and session-management reference, and linked update docs warning to the doctor section. Validation: pnpm docs:check-mdx; pnpm format:docs:check.

Worker 5: confirmed the cron isolated-agent message-tool-policy regression is fixed at this branch head. Focused proof passed: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts (53 tests).
